If snap.publicId, async destroys via cloudinary.uploader.destroy(snap.publicId, {resource_type:snap.mediaType=='video'?'video':'image'}) (non-blocking). Deletes snap doc. Response 200 {success:true, message:'Snap deleted'}. Error 404.

files.purpose: HTTP handlers for toggling bookmarks, retrieving bookmarks, checking bookmark status
files.details:
toggleBookmark: POST /api/v1/bookmarks, body {itemId, itemType}. Validates both present (400), itemType in ['post','reel'] (400). Finds Bookmark {user:userId, itemId, itemType}. If exists, deletes it, if itemType=='reel', decrements Reel.stats.saves via $inc. Returns 200 {success:true, data:{isBookmarked:false}}. If not exists, creates Bookmark {user:userId, itemId, itemType}, if itemType=='reel', increments Reel.stats.saves via $inc. Returns 201 {success:true, data:{isBookmarked:true}}. Errors: 400 if missing/invalid itemType, 500.

getBookmarks: GET /api/v1/bookmarks?type=post|reel&page=1&limit=20. Query type ('post'|'reel', default 'post'), page (default 1), limit (default 20). Finds bookmarks {user:userId, itemType:type}, sorts {createdAt:-1}, skips/limits. If type=='post', fetches Post {_id:{$in:itemIds}, isActive:true}, populates author, maps {post, isLiked:post.likes?.some(id=>id.toString()==userId.toString())||false, isBookmarked:true}. If type=='reel', fetches Reel {_id:{$in:itemIds}}, populates user, maps {reel, isSaved:true}. itemMap, orders by Bookmark.createdAt. Response 200 {success:true, data:items[], pagination:{page, limit}}.

checkBookmark: GET /api/v1/bookmarks/check/:itemId. Finds Bookmark {user:userId, itemId}. Response 200 {success:true, data:{isBookmarked:!!exists}}.

files.purpose: Empty stub file
files.details: File contains 0 lines. No handlers defined. Comment functionality colocated in postController and reelController.

specs.overview: Social and messaging controllers subsystem for Pulse. Handles six core social features: direct messaging (DMs) and group conversations, user notifications with filtering and unread tracking, location-based anonymous whisper comments, random 1-on-1 roulette matching with timed chats, and collaborative chain storytelling. All endpoints are authenticated and enforce authorization checks. Controllers delegate business logic to model static/instance methods. Database uses MongoDB with Mongoose; caching uses Redis for follow relationships and feed invalidation. All responses follow the { success, data/message, pagination? } convention with specific HTTP status codes per operation.

files.purpose: Handles direct message (DM) conversations: creating/retrieving conversations, fetching message history with pagination, marking conversations as read, soft-deleting messages, and searching conversations.
files.schema: Conversation schema: type (enum: direct|group), participants (array of User refs), groupName, groupAvatar, groupDescription, admins (array of User refs), createdBy (User ref), lastMessage (Message ref), lastMessageContent, lastMessageSender, lastMessageAt, unreadCounts (Map of userId → count). Message schema: conversation (Conversation ref), sender (User ref), type (enum: text|image|video|gif|sticker|system), content, media (object with url, thumbnail, width, height, mimeType), replyTo (Message ref), reactions (Map of userId → emoji), readBy (array of User refs), isDeleted (boolean). Indexes: Conversation {participants:1,lastMessageAt:-1}, Message {conversation:1,createdAt:-1}.
files.details:
=== getOrCreateConversation ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chat/conversation
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - targetUserId (string, required): The user ID to create DM with

Validation:
  - targetUserId must be provided; returns 400 if missing

Database Operations:
  1. Query Conversation collection: findOne({ type: 'direct', participants: { $all: [currentUserId, targetUserId], $size: 2 } })
     - Populate participants with fields: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified, isOnline
  2. If not found, create new Conversation:
     - type: 'direct'
     - participants: [currentUserId, targetUserId]
     - unreadCounts: { [currentUserId]: 0, [targetUserId]: 0 }
  3. Populate the created conversation with participants data

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: conversation_object }
  conversation_object fields: _id, type, participants (populated), unreadCounts, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== getConversations ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chat/conversations
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - page (integer, optional, default=1): Page number for pagination, minimum 1
  - limit (integer, optional, default=50): Items per page, min 1, max 100 (enforced with Math.min/Math.max)

Database Operations:
  1. Query Conversation collection: find({ participants: req.user.userId })
  2. Sort by { lastMessageAt: -1 } (newest conversations first)
  3. Pagination: skip((page - 1) * limit).limit(limit)
  4. Populate:
     - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified, isOnline
     - admins: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
     - createdBy: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
  5. Use .lean() for read-only optimization

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [conversation_objects],
    pagination: { page, limit, hasMore: conversations.length === limit }
  }
  conversation_objects include all fields (both DM and group fields)

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== getMessages ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chat/:conversationId/messages
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - conversationId (string, required): The conversation ID

Query Parameters:
  - limit (integer, optional, default=50): Number of messages to return
  - before (ISO 8601 datetime, optional): If provided, fetch messages before this timestamp (for pagination)

Authorization:
  - User must be a participant in the conversation; returns 403 if not

Database Operations:
  1. Verify authorization: findOne({ _id: conversationId, participants: req.user.userId })
  2. Build query for Message collection:
     - conversation: conversationId
     - isDeleted: { $ne: true }
     - If 'before' provided: createdAt: { $lt: new Date(before) }
  3. Find and sort: find(query).sort({ createdAt: -1 }).limit(parseInt(limit))
  4. Populate:
     - sender: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified
     - replyTo (nested populate):
       - Select: content, sender, type, media
       - Populate sender: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
  5. Use .lean() for optimization

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: [message_objects] }
  message_objects: _id, conversation, sender (populated), type, content, media, replyTo (populated), reactions, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 403: { success: false, message: "Not authorized" }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

Notes:
  - Messages are soft-deleted (isDeleted flag, not removed from DB)
  - Messages sorted newest-first to support reverse chronological loading
  - 'before' pagination enables cursor-based pagination for chat history

---

=== markConversationRead ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chat/:conversationId/read
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - conversationId (string, required): The conversation ID

Authorization:
  - User must be a participant in the conversation

Database Operations:
  1. findOneAndUpdate on Conversation collection:
     - Filter: { _id: conversationId, participants: userId }
     - Update: { $set: { [`unreadCounts.${userId}`]: 0 } }
     - Returns the updated document
  2. If no document matched, authorization failed

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Conversation marked as read' }

Error Responses:
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Updates unreadCounts map for the current user in the conversation

---

=== deleteMessage ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: DELETE /api/v1/chat/messages/:messageId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - messageId (string, required): The message ID to delete

Authorization:
  - Only the message sender can delete their own message

Database Operations:
  1. Query Message collection: findOne({ _id: messageId, sender: userId })
  2. If found, soft-delete via findByIdAndUpdate:
     - Update: { isDeleted: true, content: 'This message was deleted' }

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Message deleted' }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Message not found or unauthorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Message marked as deleted but not removed from DB
  - Content replaced with placeholder text

---

=== getConversationDetails ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chat/:conversationId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - conversationId (string, required): The conversation ID

Authorization:
  - User must be a participant in the conversation

Database Operations:
  1. findOne on Conversation collection:
     - Filter: { _id: conversationId, participants: req.user.userId }
     - Populate:
       - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified, isOnline
       - admins: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
       - createdBy: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: conversation_object }
  Includes all Conversation fields (type, groupName, groupAvatar, groupDescription, admins, createdBy, etc.)

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Conversation not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== searchConversations ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chat/search
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - q (string, required): Search query for conversation names

Validation:
  - If q is empty or whitespace-only, returns empty array

Database Operations:
  1. Build query object:
     - participants: req.user.userId
     - $or: [{ groupName: { $regex: escapeRegex(q.trim()), $options: 'i' } }]
  2. Sort by { lastMessageAt: -1 }
  3. Limit to 20 results
  4. Populate:
     - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified
     - admins: username
  5. Use .lean() for optimization

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: [conversation_objects] }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Notes:
  - Uses escapeRegex utility to sanitize user input and prevent ReDoS attacks
  - Case-insensitive search via $options: 'i'
  - Searches only group names, not participant usernames (requires aggregation for full-text search)

Side Effects: None

files.purpose: Handles group conversation management: create groups, add/remove members, admin promotion/demotion, group info updates, deletion, and leave functionality. Creates system messages for all group actions.
files.schema: Uses Conversation model with type='group'. Additional group-only fields: groupName (string, maxlength 100), groupAvatar (string, nullable), groupDescription (string, maxlength 500), admins (array of User refs), createdBy (User ref). Indexes: {type:1}, {participants:1,lastMessageAt:-1}.
files.details:
=== createGroup ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/groups
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - groupName (string, required): Name of the group (maxlength 100)
  - participants (array, required): Array of user IDs; must have at least 2 members
  - groupDescription (string, optional): Group description (maxlength 500)
  - groupAvatar (string, optional): Group avatar URL

Validation:
  - groupName must be provided and non-empty
  - participants must be an array with at least 2 elements
  - Returns 400 if validation fails

Database Operations:
  1. Deduplicate and add creator to participants if not included: [...new Set([creatorId, ...participants])]
  2. Initialize unreadCounts map with all participant IDs set to 0
  3. Create Conversation document:
     - type: 'group'
     - groupName, groupDescription, groupAvatar
     - participants: allParticipants array
     - admins: [creatorId]
     - createdBy: creatorId
     - lastMessageContent: "${username} created the group"
     - unreadCounts: initialized map
  4. Create system Message:
     - conversation: group._id
     - sender: creatorId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${username} created \"${groupName}\""
  5. Populate and return the created group:
     - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified
     - admins: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
     - createdBy: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar

Response (201):
  { success: true, data: group_object }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Group name and at least 2 participants required' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Creates system message in the group conversation

---

=== getGroupDetails ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Authorization:
  - User must be a participant in the group

Database Operations:
  1. findOne on Conversation collection:
     - Filter: { _id: groupId, type: 'group', participants: req.user.userId }
     - Populate:
       - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified, isOnline
       - admins: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar
       - createdBy: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: group_object }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Group not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== addGroupMembers ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/groups/:groupId/members
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Request Body:
  - userIds (array of strings, required): User IDs to add to the group

Validation:
  - userIds must be a non-empty array
  - Returns 400 if not provided or empty

Authorization:
  - User making the request must be an admin of the group
  - Returns 403 if not admin

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', admins: req.user.userId })
  2. Calculate new members: filter(id => !group.participants.includes(id))
  3. If no new members, return 400
  4. Update Conversation:
     - $addToSet: { participants: { $each: newMembers } }
  5. Initialize unread counts for new members:
     - $set: { [`unreadCounts.${id}`]: 0 } for each new member
  6. Fetch User data: find({ _id: { $in: newMembers } }).select('username')
  7. Create system Message:
     - conversation: groupId
     - sender: req.user.userId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${req.user.username} added ${usernames_joined_by_comma}"
  8. Populate updated group:
     - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: updated_group_object }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'User IDs required' | 'All users are already members' }
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized or group not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Adds participants to group
  - Initializes unread counts for new members
  - Creates system message

---

=== removeGroupMember ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: DELETE /api/v1/groups/:groupId/members/:userId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID
  - userId (string, required): The user ID to remove

Authorization:
  - User making the request must be an admin
  - Returns 403 if not admin

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', admins: req.user.userId })
  2. Validation: String(group.createdBy) must not equal String(userId)
     - Returns 400 if attempting to remove group creator
  3. Update Conversation:
     - $pull: { participants: userId, admins: userId }
  4. Fetch removed user: findById(userId).select('username')
  5. Create system Message:
     - conversation: groupId
     - sender: req.user.userId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${req.user.username} removed ${removed_user.username}"

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Member removed' }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Cannot remove group creator' }
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Removes user from participants and admins
  - Creates system message

---

=== leaveGroup ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/groups/:groupId/leave
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Authorization:
  - User must be a participant in the group
  - Creator cannot leave (must delete group instead)
  - Returns 400 if user is the creator

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', participants: userId })
  2. Validation: String(group.createdBy) must not equal String(userId)
  3. Update Conversation:
     - $pull: { participants: userId, admins: userId }
  4. Create system Message:
     - conversation: groupId
     - sender: userId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${req.user.username} left the group"

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Left group successfully' }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Group not found' }
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Group creator cannot leave. Delete the group instead.' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Removes user from participants and admins
  - Creates system message

---

=== updateGroupInfo ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: PUT /api/v1/groups/:groupId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Request Body (at least one of):
  - groupName (string, optional): New group name
  - groupDescription (string, optional): New group description
  - groupAvatar (string, optional): New group avatar URL

Authorization:
  - User must be an admin of the group

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', admins: req.user.userId })
  2. Build updates object from body (only include provided fields)
  3. Update Conversation:
     - $set: updates
     - new: true to return updated document
  4. Populate updated group:
     - participants: username, name, avatar, profile.avatar, isVerified
  5. If groupName changed (compare with original):
     - Create system Message:
       - conversation: groupId
       - sender: req.user.userId
       - type: 'system'
       - content: "${req.user.username} changed the group name to \"${groupName}\""

Response (200):
  { success: true, data: updated_group_object }

Error Responses:
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Updates group metadata
  - Creates system message if name changed

---

=== makeAdmin ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/groups/:groupId/admins
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Request Body:
  - userId (string, required): User ID to promote to admin

Authorization:
  - Only the group creator can make admins
  - Returns 403 if not creator

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', createdBy: req.user.userId })
  2. Validation: Check if userId is a participant
     - Returns 400 if not a member
  3. Update Conversation:
     - $addToSet: { admins: userId }
  4. Fetch user: findById(userId).select('username')
  5. Create system Message:
     - conversation: groupId
     - sender: req.user.userId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${user.username} is now an admin"

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Admin added' }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'User is not a group member' }
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Adds user to admins array
  - Creates system message

---

=== removeAdmin ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: DELETE /api/v1/groups/:groupId/admins/:userId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID
  - userId (string, required): User ID to demote from admin

Authorization:
  - Only the group creator can remove admins

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', createdBy: req.user.userId })
  2. Validation: String(group.createdBy) must not equal String(userId)
     - Returns 400 if attempting to remove creator as admin
  3. Update Conversation:
     - $pull: { admins: userId }
  4. Fetch user: findById(userId).select('username')
  5. Create system Message:
     - conversation: groupId
     - sender: req.user.userId
     - type: 'system'
     - content: "${user.username} is no longer an admin"

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Admin removed' }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Cannot remove creator as admin' }
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Removes user from admins array
  - Creates system message

---

=== deleteGroup ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: DELETE /api/v1/groups/:groupId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - groupId (string, required): The group conversation ID

Authorization:
  - Only the group creator can delete
  - Returns 403 if not creator

Database Operations:
  1. Query: findOne({ _id: groupId, type: 'group', createdBy: req.user.userId })
  2. Delete all messages: deleteMany({ conversation: groupId })
  3. Delete conversation: findByIdAndDelete(groupId)

Response (200):
  { success: true, message: 'Group deleted' }

Error Responses:
  - 403: { success: false, message: 'Not authorized or group not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Deletes entire conversation and all associated messages

Notes:
  - This is a hard delete (not soft)
  - All message history is destroyed

files.purpose: Manages user notifications: fetch paginated notifications with filtering, get unread counts by type, mark individual/all notifications as read, and delete notifications.
files.schema: Notification schema: recipient (User ref, indexed), sender (User ref), type (enum: like|comment|follow|chat|whisper|mention|reel_like|reel_comment), post (Post ref), reel (Reel ref), whisper (Whisper ref), conversation (Conversation ref), comment (Comment ref), message (string, maxlength 200), isRead (boolean, indexed). Indexes: {recipient:1,createdAt:-1}, {recipient:1,isRead:1,createdAt:-1}, {recipient:1,type:1,createdAt:-1}. Static methods: getNotifications(), getUnreadCountByType(), markAsRead(), markAllAsRead(), createNotification() (with duplicate prevention and async push notification), deleteOldNotifications().
files.details:
=== getNotifications ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/notifications
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - page (integer, optional, default=1): Page number for pagination
  - limit (integer, optional, default=20): Items per page
  - type (string, optional): Filter by notification type (enum: 'like', 'comment', 'follow', 'chat', 'whisper', 'mention', 'reel_like', 'reel_comment')
  - unreadOnly (boolean as string, optional): If 'true', return only unread notifications

Database Operations:
  1. Call Notification.getNotifications(userId, options) static method with:
     - page: parseInt(page)
     - limit: parseInt(limit)
     - type: type || null
     - unreadOnly: unreadOnly === 'true'
  2. Static method performs:
     - Build query: { recipient: userId, [type filter], [isRead: false if unreadOnly] }
     - find(query).sort({ createdAt: -1 })
     - skip((page - 1) * limit).limit(limit)
     - Populate: sender (username, profile.displayName, profile.avatar, isVerified)
     - Populate: post (content.text, content.media)
     - Populate: reel (caption, videoUrl)
     - Use .lean()
     - countDocuments(query) for total
     - Return { notifications, pagination: { page, limit, total, hasMore } }

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [notification_objects],
    pagination: { page, limit, total, hasMore: page * limit < total }
  }
  notification_objects: _id, recipient, sender (populated), type, post (populated), reel (populated), whisper, conversation, comment, message, isRead, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: 'Failed to fetch notifications' }

Side Effects: None

---

=== getUnreadCount ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/notifications/unread
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters: None

Database Operations:
  1. Call Notification.getUnreadCountByType(userId) static method
  2. Static method performs aggregation:
     - $match: { recipient: userId (as ObjectId), isRead: false }
     - $group: { _id: '$type', count: { $sum: 1 } }
  3. Transform result into counts object:
     - counts = { total: 0 }
     - For each aggregation result: counts[type] = count; counts.total += count
     - Return counts

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: { total: number, [type]: count, ... }
  }
  Example: { total: 5, like: 2, comment: 1, follow: 2 }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: 'Failed to get unread count' }

Side Effects: None

---

=== markAsRead ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/notifications/:id/read
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - id (string, required): The notification ID

Database Operations:
  1. Call Notification.markAsRead(notificationId, userId) static method
  2. Static method performs:
     - findOneAndUpdate({
         _id: notificationId,
         recipient: userId
       },
       { isRead: true },
       { new: true }
     )
     - Returns updated notification or null

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: notification_object
  }
  notification_object includes isRead: true

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Notification not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: 'Failed to mark notification as read' }

Side Effects:
  - Sets isRead to true for the notification

---

=== markAllAsRead ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/notifications/mark-all-read
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - type (string, optional): If provided, mark only notifications of this type as read

Database Operations:
  1. Call Notification.markAllAsRead(userId, type || null) static method
  2. Static method performs:
     - Build query: { recipient: userId, isRead: false, [type filter if provided] }
     - updateMany(query, { isRead: true })
     - Returns { modifiedCount: number }

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    message: 'All notifications marked as read'
  }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: 'Failed to mark notifications as read' }

Side Effects:
  - Sets isRead to true for all matching unread notifications

---

=== deleteNotification ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: DELETE /api/v1/notifications/:id
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - id (string, required): The notification ID to delete

Database Operations:
  1. Direct call to Notification.findOneAndDelete({ _id: id, recipient: userId })
     - Ensures user can only delete their own notifications
     - Returns deleted document or null

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    message: 'Notification deleted'
  }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Notification not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: 'Failed to delete notification' }

Side Effects:
  - Hard deletes notification from database

Notes:
  - Notification model has deduplication logic: prevents duplicate notifications of same type from same sender within 60 seconds
  - Notifications trigger async push notifications (non-blocking, fire-and-forget via setImmediate)
  - Push notification service called asynchronously; failures don't affect API response

files.purpose: Handles location-based anonymous social features: fetch nearby whispers with geospatial queries, create/vote on/reply to whispers, and report inappropriate content. All whisper authors are anonymized in responses.
files.schema: Whisper schema: content (string, maxlength 280, required, trimmed), location (GeoJSON Point with [lng, lat] coordinates, 2dsphere indexed), city, region, author (User ref, required, select: false to exclude by default), upvotes, downvotes, score (upvotes - downvotes), voters (array of {user (User ref, select: false), vote (enum: up|down)}), replies (array of {content, author (select: false), upvotes, createdAt}), reports (count), isHidden (boolean), expiresAt (TTL: 24 hours, auto-cleanup). Indexes: location 2dsphere, {score:-1,createdAt:-1}. Static methods: getNearby(lng, lat, radius, limit), vote(whisperId, userId, voteType). Instance methods: addReply(content, authorId).
files.details:
=== getNearby ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/whispers/nearby
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - lng (number, required): Longitude coordinate
  - lat (number, required): Latitude coordinate
  - radius (number, optional, default=5): Search radius in kilometers
  - limit (number, optional, default=50): Maximum whispers to return

Validation:
  - lng and lat are required; returns 400 if missing

Database Operations:
  1. Call Whisper.getNearby(lng, lat, radius, limit) static method
  2. Static method performs geospatial query:
     - location: { $near: { $geometry: { type: 'Point', coordinates: [lng, lat] }, $maxDistance: radius * 1000 } }
     - isHidden: false (exclude hidden whispers)
     - sort({ score: -1, createdAt: -1 })
     - limit(limit)
     - select('-author -voters.user -replies.author') (exclude author info)
  3. For each returned whisper, calculate distance using Haversine formula:
     - const R = 6371 (Earth's radius in km)
     - dLat = (lat2 - lat1) * Math.PI / 180
     - dLng = (lng2 - lng1) * Math.PI / 180
     - a = sin²(dLat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin²(dLng/2)
     - c = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a))
     - distance = R * c

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [whisper_objects_with_distance]
  }
  whisper_objects: _id, content, location, city, region, upvotes, downvotes, score, replies (array), reports, isHidden, expiresAt, createdAt, updatedAt, distance (string formatted as "X.X km")
  NOTE: author field is NEVER included in response

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Location coordinates required' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== create ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/whispers
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - content (string, required): The whisper content (maxlength 280), must be trimmed non-empty
  - lng (number, required): Longitude
  - lat (number, required): Latitude
  - city (string, optional): City name for context
  - region (string, optional): Region/state name for context

Validation:
  - content must be non-empty after trim; returns 400 if missing
  - lng and lat required; returns 400 if missing

Database Operations:
  1. Create Whisper document:
     - content: content.trim()
     - author: userId
     - location: { type: 'Point', coordinates: [parseFloat(lng), parseFloat(lat)] }
     - city, region
  2. Convert to plain object and remove author field before returning

Response (201):
  {
    success: true,
    data: whisper_object_without_author
  }
  whisper_object: _id, content, location, city, region, upvotes: 0, downvotes: 0, score: 0, replies: [], reports: 0, isHidden: false, expiresAt (24 hours from now), createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Content is required' | 'Location is required' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Creates new whisper document
  - Auto-expires after 24 hours via TTL index

Notes:
  - Author is stored but never exposed to client
  - Whisper is automatically anonymous to end users

---

=== vote ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/whispers/:whisperId/vote
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - whisperId (string, required): The whisper ID

Request Body:
  - voteType (string, required): Either 'up' or 'down'

Validation:
  - voteType must be 'up' or 'down'; returns 400 otherwise

Database Operations:
  1. Call Whisper.vote(whisperId, userId, voteType) static method
  2. Static method performs:
     - findById(whisperId).select('+voters.user')
     - Check existing vote: findIndex(v => v.user === userId)
     - If existing vote same as new: remove vote (toggle off)
       - Decrement corresponding upvotes or downvotes
       - Remove from voters array
     - If existing vote different: change vote
       - Decrement old vote counter
       - Update vote type
       - Increment new vote counter
     - If no existing vote: add new vote
       - voters.push({ user: userId, vote: voteType })
       - Increment corresponding counter
     - Recalculate score = upvotes - downvotes
     - save()
     - Return { upvotes, downvotes, score }

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: { upvotes: number, downvotes: number, score: number }
  }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Invalid vote type' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Updates upvotes/downvotes counts
  - Updates voters array
  - Recalculates score

Notes:
  - Voting is idempotent; voting same direction twice removes the vote
  - Allows vote switching (change from upvote to downvote)

---

=== reply ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/whispers/:whisperId/reply
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - whisperId (string, required): The whisper ID

Request Body:
  - content (string, required): Reply content (maxlength 200)

Database Operations:
  1. findById(whisperId) and verify exists; returns 404 if not found
  2. Call whisper.addReply(content, userId) instance method
  3. Instance method performs:
     - replies.push({ content, author: userId, upvotes: 0, createdAt: now })
     - save()
     - Return the added reply object
  4. Convert reply to plain object and delete author field

Response (201):
  {
    success: true,
    data: reply_object_without_author
  }
  reply_object: content, upvotes: 0, createdAt

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Whisper not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Adds reply to whisper's replies array
  - Author information hidden in response

Notes:
  - Replies are also anonymous
  - Stored with author ID internally but never exposed

---

=== report ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/whispers/:whisperId/report
Authentication: req.user.userId required (not used for authorization, any user can report)

Path Parameters:
  - whisperId (string, required): The whisper ID

Database Operations:
  1. findById(whisperId); returns 404 if not found
  2. Increment reports counter: whisper.reports++
  3. If reports >= 5: set isHidden = true
  4. save()

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    message: 'Reported'
  }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Whisper not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Increments report count
  - Hides whisper after 5 reports
  - Once hidden, whisper excluded from nearby queries (isHidden: false filter)

Notes:
  - Simple report counter; no duplicate prevention
  - Anyone can report any whisper
  - No report reason or metadata stored

files.purpose: Manages random 1-on-1 matching for timed chats: queue management, match finding, message exchange, mutual connection decisions, and chat history. Mutual connects create follow relationships.
files.schema: Roulette schema: users (array of {user (User ref), joinedAt, decision (enum: pending|connect|pass)}), status (enum: waiting|matched|chatting|deciding|completed|expired), matchScore, matchReason, messages (array of {sender (User ref), text (maxlength 500), timestamp}), matchedAt, chatStartedAt, chatDuration (seconds, default 180), expiresAt, outcome (enum: mutual_connect|one_sided|mutual_pass|expired), icebreaker (string). Indexes: {status:1}, {'users.user':1}, {expiresAt:1} TTL. Static methods: joinQueue(userId), findMatch(userId), getUserHistory(userId, limit). Instance methods: startChat(), addMessage(senderId, text), recordDecision(userId, decision).
files.details:
=== joinQueue ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/roulette/join
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body: None

Database Operations:
  1. Call Roulette.findMatch(userId) to attempt immediate match
  2. If match found:
     - Call match.startChat() to transition status from 'matched' to 'chatting'
     - Populate match with users.user (username, profile.displayName, profile.avatar)
     - Find partner: users.find(u => u.user._id !== userId)?.user
     - Return success with status 'matched'
  3. If no match found:
     - Call Roulette.joinQueue(userId) static method
     - Static method:
       - Check for existing active session: findOne({ 'users.user': userId, status: { $in: ['waiting', 'matched', 'chatting', 'deciding'] } })
       - If exists, return existing session
       - Otherwise create new Roulette document with status 'waiting'
     - Return success with status 'waiting'

Response (200):
  If matched immediately:
  {
    success: true,
    data: {
      sessionId: string,
      status: 'matched',
      partner: user_object,
      icebreaker: string,
      chatDuration: number (seconds)
    }
  }
  If waiting for match:
  {
    success: true,
    data: {
      sessionId: string,
      status: 'waiting',
      message: 'Looking for someone to match with...'
    }
  }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to join roulette' }

Matching Logic:
  - Roulette.findMatch(userId) finds waiting sessions:
    - status: 'waiting'
    - 'users.user': { $ne: userId } (exclude current user)
    - 'users.0.joinedAt': { $gte: new Date(Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000) } (within 5 minutes)
  - Sort by earliest joinedAt and take first (longest waiting user)
  - Combine into one session: match.users.push({ user: userId, joinedAt: now })
  - Set status: 'matched'
  - Set matchedAt: now
  - Select random icebreaker from ICEBREAKERS array (15 prompts)
  - Set expiresAt: now + 15 minutes (TTL for cleanup)

Side Effects:
  - Creates waiting session if no match found
  - Creates/updates Roulette document
  - Sets TTL expiry for auto-cleanup

Notes:
  - Icebreaker prompts are predefined (15 options with emojis)
  - Default chat duration: 180 seconds (3 minutes)
  - Sessions auto-expire after 15 minutes total (from match or creation)

---

=== checkStatus ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/roulette/status
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters: None

Database Operations:
  1. Query Roulette: findOne({
       'users.user': userId,
       status: { $in: ['waiting', 'matched', 'chatting', 'deciding'] }
     })
  2. Populate users.user with username, profile.displayName, profile.avatar
  3. If no session found, return status 'none'
  4. If session found:
     - Find partner: users.find(u => u.user._id !== userId)?.user
     - Calculate time remaining if status 'chatting':
       - elapsed = (Date.now() - chatStartedAt.getTime()) / 1000
       - timeRemaining = Math.max(0, chatDuration - Math.floor(elapsed))
       - If timeRemaining <= 0 and status === 'chatting':
         - Set status to 'deciding'
         - save()

Response (200):
  If no active session:
  {
    success: true,
    data: { status: 'none' }
  }
  If session exists:
  {
    success: true,
    data: {
      sessionId: string,
      status: 'waiting' | 'matched' | 'chatting' | 'deciding',
      partner: user_object | null,
      icebreaker: string,
      messages: [message_objects] (last 50),
      timeRemaining: number | null (seconds remaining, only if chatting),
      chatDuration: number (seconds),
      outcome: string | null
    }
  }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to check status' }

Side Effects:
  - Auto-transitions status from 'chatting' to 'deciding' when time expires
  - Persists status change to database

Notes:
  - Messages returned are last 50 from the array
  - timeRemaining only populated for 'chatting' status
  - Client should poll this endpoint to track progress

---

=== sendMessage ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/roulette/message
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - sessionId (string, required): The roulette session ID
  - text (string, required): Message text, maxlength 500

Validation:
  - text must be trimmed non-empty; returns 400 if missing

Database Operations:
  1. findById(sessionId)
  2. Verify user is a participant: session.users.find(u => u.user === userId)
  3. Verify status is 'chatting'; returns 400 if not
  4. Call session.addMessage(userId, text.trim()) instance method
  5. Instance method performs:
     - messages.push({ sender: userId, text: text.substring(0, 500), timestamp: now })
     - If messages.length > 100: messages = messages.slice(-100) (keep last 100)
     - save()
     - Return the added message object

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: message_object
  }
  message_object: sender (ObjectId), text, timestamp

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, error: 'Message text required' | 'Chat time is over' }
  - 404: { success: false, error: 'Session not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to send message' }

Side Effects:
  - Adds message to session
  - Caps messages at 100 (oldest removed when exceeding limit)

Notes:
  - Messages are not stored separately; embedded in Roulette document
  - Text truncated to 500 chars server-side
  - Only possible during 'chatting' status

---

=== decide ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/roulette/decide
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - sessionId (string, required): The roulette session ID
  - decision (string, required): Either 'connect' or 'pass'

Validation:
  - decision must be 'connect' or 'pass'; returns 400 otherwise

Database Operations:
  1. findById(sessionId)
  2. Verify user is a participant
  3. Call session.recordDecision(userId, decision) instance method
  4. Instance method performs:
     - Find user entry in users array
     - Set decision to 'connect' or 'pass'
     - Check if all users have decided: every(u => u.decision !== 'pending')
     - If all decided:
       - Set status to 'completed'
       - Evaluate decisions to set outcome:
         - If all 'connect': outcome = 'mutual_connect'
         - If all 'pass': outcome = 'mutual_pass'
         - Otherwise: outcome = 'one_sided'
     - Else: set status to 'deciding'
     - save()
     - Return { outcome, status }
  5. If outcome is 'mutual_connect':
     - Get other user ID from users array
     - Create mutual follow records (idempotent with unique index):
       - Follow.create({ follower: userId, following: otherUserId })
       - Follow.create({ follower: otherUserId, following: userId })
       - Catch duplicate key error (code 11000) and ignore
     - Reconcile user stats from Follow collection counts:
       - Follow.getFollowerCount(userId), Follow.getFollowingCount(userId)
       - Follow.getFollowerCount(otherUserId), Follow.getFollowingCount(otherUserId)
     - Update User documents:
       - User.updateOne({ _id: userId }, { $set: { 'stats.followers': count, 'stats.following': count } })
       - User.updateOne({ _id: otherUserId }, { $set: { 'stats.followers': count, 'stats.following': count } })
     - Clear cache keys (async, non-blocking):
       - cacheService.del(`followgraph:${userId}`)
       - cacheService.del(`followgraph:${otherUserId}`)
       - cacheService.del(`reel:following:${userId}`)
       - cacheService.del(`reel:following:${otherUserId}`)

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: result
  }
  result: { outcome: string | null, status: 'deciding' | 'completed' }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, error: 'Decision must be "connect" or "pass"' }
  - 404: { success: false, error: 'Session not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to record decision' }

Side Effects:
  - Records decision in session
  - Updates session status
  - If mutual connect: creates bidirectional follow relationships
  - If mutual connect: updates User stats.followers and stats.following
  - If mutual connect: invalidates related cache keys

Notes:
  - Follow creation is idempotent via unique compound index (follower, following)
  - Concurrent mutual connects don't cause duplicates or stat increments
  - Cache invalidation is non-blocking (fire-and-forget)
  - Errors in follow creation logged but don't fail API response

---

=== leave ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/roulette/leave
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body: None

Database Operations:
  1. Query Roulette: findOne({ 'users.user': userId, status: { $in: ['waiting', 'matched'] } })
  2. If session found:
     - Set status to 'expired'
     - Set outcome to 'expired'
     - save()

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    message: 'Left roulette'
  }

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to leave' }

Side Effects:
  - Terminates waiting or matched session
  - Can only leave if not in 'chatting' or 'deciding' status

Notes:
  - Only exits waiting or matched sessions
  - Cannot leave during active chat or decision phase

---

=== getHistory ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/roulette/history
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - limit (integer, optional, default=20): Maximum sessions to return

Database Operations:
  1. Call Roulette.getUserHistory(userId, limit) static method
  2. Static method performs:
     - find({ 'users.user': userId, status: 'completed' })
     - sort({ createdAt: -1 })
     - limit(limit)
     - populate('users.user', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar')
     - lean()
  3. Transform results:
     - For each history item:
       - sessionId: h._id
       - partner: h.users.find(u => u.user._id?.toString() !== userId)?.user
       - outcome: h.outcome
       - messageCount: h.messages?.length || 0
       - date: h.createdAt

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [history_objects]
  }
  history_objects: sessionId, partner, outcome, messageCount, date

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, error: 'Failed to get history' }

Side Effects: None

Notes:
  - Only includes completed sessions
  - Partner info excludes user themselves
  - Outcome can be 'mutual_connect', 'one_sided', 'mutual_pass', or 'expired'

files.purpose: Manages collaborative storytelling: browse/create chains, submit story segments, vote on pending segments for approval, and like chains. Tracks contributors and requires threshold votes for segment acceptance.
files.schema: ChainStory schema: title (string, maxlength 80, required), previewImage (string), starterContent (string, maxlength 500, required), starterAuthor (User ref, required), segments (array of segment subdocs), pendingSegments (array of segment subdocs), segmentCount, contributorCount, contributors (array of User refs), totalVotes, likes, status (enum: active|complete|archived), maxSegments (default 50), isPublic (default true), allowAnyone (default true), requireVotes (number, default 3), genre (enum: mystery|comedy|drama|horror|romance|adventure|other), tags. Segment subdoc: content (maxlength 500), media (string), mediaType (enum: image|video|none), author (User ref), votes, voters (array of {user, value}), isApproved. Indexes: {status:1,likes:-1}, {genre:1}, {'segments.author':1}.
files.details:
=== getChains ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chains
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Query Parameters:
  - page (integer, optional, default=1): Page number for pagination
  - limit (integer, optional, default=20): Items per page
  - status (string, optional, default='active'): Filter by status ('active', 'complete', 'archived', or 'all')
  - genre (string, optional): Filter by genre enum ('mystery', 'comedy', 'drama', 'horror', 'romance', 'adventure', 'other')

Database Operations:
  1. Build query object:
     - If status !== 'all': query.status = status
     - If genre provided: query.genre = genre
  2. Query ChainStory collection: find(query)
  3. Sort by { likes: -1, contributorCount: -1 }
  4. Paginate: skip((page - 1) * limit).limit(parseInt(limit))
  5. Populate starterAuthor with username, profile.avatar
  6. Select all except pendingSegments and segments.voters
  7. Transform results:
     - Add lastSegment virtual:
       - If segments.length > 0: lastSegment = segments[length-1].content
       - Else: lastSegment = starterContent

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [chain_objects_with_lastSegment]
  }
  chain_objects: _id, title, previewImage, starterContent, starterAuthor (populated), status, genre, tags, likes, contributorCount, segmentCount, maxSegments, requireVotes, lastSegment, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

---

=== getById ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chains/:chainId
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - chainId (string, required): The chain story ID

Database Operations:
  1. findById(chainId)
  2. Populate:
     - starterAuthor: username, profile.avatar
     - segments.author: username, profile.avatar
     - contributors: username, profile.avatar

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: chain_object
  }
  chain_object: _id, title, previewImage, starterContent, starterAuthor (populated), segments (populated with author), pendingSegments, contributors (populated), status, genre, tags, likes, contributorCount, segmentCount, maxSegments, requireVotes, isPublic, allowAnyone, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Chain not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

Notes:
  - Returns full chain with segments and pending segments
  - Segments include voter information

---

=== create ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chains
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Request Body:
  - title (string, required): Chain title (maxlength 80), must be trimmed non-empty
  - starterContent (string, required): Initial story prompt (maxlength 500), must be trimmed non-empty
  - genre (string, optional, default='other'): One of enum values
  - previewImage (string, optional): Preview image URL
  - maxSegments (integer, optional, default=50): Maximum segments allowed
  - requireVotes (integer, optional, default=3): Votes needed to approve segment

Validation:
  - title and starterContent required and non-empty after trim; returns 400 otherwise

Database Operations:
  1. Create ChainStory document:
     - title: title.trim()
     - starterContent: starterContent.trim()
     - starterAuthor: userId
     - genre: genre || 'other'
     - previewImage
     - maxSegments: maxSegments || 50
     - requireVotes: requireVotes || 3
     - contributors: [userId]
     - contributorCount: 1
     - status: 'active' (default)
     - isPublic: true (default)
     - allowAnyone: true (default)

Response (201):
  {
    success: true,
    data: chain_object
  }
  chain_object: _id, title, starterContent, starterAuthor, status, genre, contributors, contributorCount, segments: [], pendingSegments: [], likes: 0, createdAt, updatedAt

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Title and starter content required' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Creator automatically added as first contributor

---

=== submitSegment ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chains/:chainId/segments
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - chainId (string, required): The chain ID

Request Body:
  - content (string, required): Segment content (maxlength 500), must be trimmed non-empty
  - media (string, optional): URL to image or video

Validation:
  - content must be non-empty after trim; returns 400 otherwise

Database Operations:
  1. findById(chainId)
  2. Verify chain exists; returns 404 if not
  3. Call chain.submitSegment(content.trim(), userId, media) instance method
  4. Instance method performs:
     - Verify status === 'active'; throws error if not
     - Verify segmentCount < maxSegments; throws error if at limit
     - Determine mediaType:
       - If media includes '.mp4': 'video'
       - Else if media: 'image'
       - Else: 'none'
     - Create segment object: { content, author: userId, media, mediaType, votes: 0, voters: [], isApproved: false, createdAt: now }
     - Push to pendingSegments array
     - save()
     - Return segment object

Response (201):
  {
    success: true,
    data: segment_object
  }
  segment_object: content, media, mediaType, author, votes: 0, voters: [], isApproved: false, createdAt

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Content required' }
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Chain not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Adds segment to pendingSegments array
  - Pending segments await voting for approval

Notes:
  - Segment submitted in pending state, not immediately added to story
  - Requires votes >= requireVotes to move to main segments array
  - Chain must be active and not at maxSegments limit

---

=== getPending ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: GET /api/v1/chains/:chainId/pending
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - chainId (string, required): The chain ID

Database Operations:
  1. findById(chainId) with select('pendingSegments title')
  2. Populate pendingSegments.author with username, profile.avatar
  3. If not found, return 404

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: [pending_segments]
  }
  pending_segments: _id, content, media, mediaType, author (populated), votes, voters, isApproved, createdAt

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Chain not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects: None

Notes:
  - Returns array of segments awaiting community votes
  - Voters array indicates who has voted and how

---

=== voteSegment ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chains/:chainId/segments/:segmentId/vote
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - chainId (string, required): The chain ID
  - segmentId (string, required): The segment ID (within pendingSegments)

Request Body:
  - value (integer, required): Vote value, must be 1 (upvote) or -1 (downvote)

Validation:
  - value must be 1 or -1; returns 400 otherwise

Database Operations:
  1. findById(chainId)
  2. Verify chain exists; returns 404 if not
  3. Call chain.voteOnSegment(segmentId, userId, value) instance method
  4. Instance method performs:
     - Get segment from pendingSegments: this.pendingSegments.id(segmentId)
     - If not found, throw error
     - Check for existing vote: findIndex(v => v.user === userId)
     - If existing vote found:
       - Subtract existing vote from votes counter
       - Remove voter from voters array
     - Add new voter: voters.push({ user: userId, value })
     - Add value to votes counter
     - Check if approved: if votes >= requireVotes and not already isApproved:
       - Set isApproved = true
       - Move segment to main segments array: this.segments.push(segment)
       - Remove from pendingSegments: this.pendingSegments.pull(segmentId)
       - Increment segmentCount
       - Add author to contributors if not already present
       - Increment contributorCount if new contributor
       - If segmentCount >= maxSegments: set status = 'complete'
     - Increment totalVotes
     - save()
     - Return { votes: segment.votes, approved: segment.isApproved }

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: { votes: number, approved: boolean }
  }

Error Responses:
  - 400: { success: false, message: 'Vote must be 1 or -1' }
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Chain not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Updates vote count for segment
  - Updates voter record
  - If threshold reached: moves segment to main story, updates contributors, may mark chain complete
  - Increments totalVotes counter

Notes:
  - Voting is not idempotent; changing vote changes the net votes
  - Segment auto-approves and moves to main story when votes >= requireVotes
  - Once approved, segment becomes permanent part of story
  - Chain auto-completes when segmentCount reaches maxSegments

---

=== likeChain ===
Signature: async (req, res)
HTTP: POST /api/v1/chains/:chainId/like
Authentication: req.user.userId required

Path Parameters:
  - chainId (string, required): The chain ID

Request Body: None

Database Operations:
  1. findById(chainId)
  2. Verify chain exists; returns 404 if not
  3. Call chain.toggleLike(userId) instance method
  4. Instance method performs:
     - this.likes++
     - save()
     - Return this.likes

Response (200):
  {
    success: true,
    data: { likes: number }
  }

Error Responses:
  - 404: { success: false, message: 'Chain not found' }
  - 500: { success: false, message: error.message }

Side Effects:
  - Increments likes counter

Notes:
  - Current implementation is simple increment; doesn't track individual user likes
  - No deduplication (same user can like multiple times)
  - May want to track likes in a separate array in production

files.purpose: Manages AI alter ego personas — creation, training, response generation, game mechanics, and activity tracking
routes: GET /me — getMyEgo — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch or create user's AlterEgo document
GET /stats — getStats — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch AlterEgo stats (totalReplies, trainingLevel, isActive, lastActive, personality, guessWhoStats, aiProvider)
PUT / — update — [verifyAccessToken] — update ego settings (name, personality, isActive, autoReplyDM, autoReplyComments)
POST /train — train — [verifyAccessToken] — update training data (howAreYou, favoriteTopics, humorStyle, complimentResponse, hotTakes, phrases, emojis)
POST /toggle — toggle — [verifyAccessToken] — toggle isActive status (requires trainingLevel >= 1)
POST /generate — generateResponse — [verifyAccessToken] — generate test response from ego to message
POST /learn — learn — [verifyAccessToken] — record trigger-response pattern for AI learning
GET /activity — getActivityLog — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch paginated activity log with pagination
POST /guess — recordGuess — [verifyAccessToken] — record guess-who game result
GET /ai-status — getAIStatus — [verifyAccessToken] — check AI provider status and availability
files.details:
## Handler: getMyEgo
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Params: none
Query: none
Logic:
  - Extract req.user.userId from auth context
  - Call AlterEgo.getOrCreate(userId) which:
    * Queries collection 'alterego' filter {user: userId}
    * If not exists, create new doc with defaults (name='My Alter Ego', trainingLevel=0)
    * save() to persist
  - Return JSON: {success: true, data: ego} where ego is full AlterEgo document
Response Status: 200 on success; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: error.message}
DB Query: AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId}); AlterEgo.create({user, name, trainingLevel})
Side Effects: Creates AlterEgo document if not exists

## Handler: update
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: {name?: string, personality?: enum['friendly','funny','professional','mysterious','chill'], isActive?: boolean, autoReplyDM?: boolean, autoReplyComments?: boolean}
Validation:
  - name: optional, any string
  - personality: optional, must be one of enum values
  - isActive: optional, must be boolean if present
  - autoReplyDM: optional, must be boolean if present
  - autoReplyComments: optional, must be boolean if present
Logic:
  - Extract userId from req.user.userId
  - Query: AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404 {success: false, message: 'Alter Ego not found'}
  - Selectively update fields present in req.body
  - Call ego.save()
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: ego}
Response Status: 200 on success; 404 if not found; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: error.message}

## Handler: train
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: {howAreYou?: string, favoriteTopics?: [string], humorStyle?: string, complimentResponse?: string, hotTakes?: [string], phrases?: [string], emojis?: [string]}
Validation: any of the above training fields
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Call ego.updateTraining(req.body) which:
    * Updates ego.training object with valid fields
    * Recalculates trainingLevel based on filled fields (0-5 levels)
    * save()
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {trainingLevel, training}}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error

## Handler: generateResponse
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: {message: string (required), context?: object}
Validation:
  - message: required, non-empty string after trim()
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Validate message is not empty
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Call ego.generateResponse(message, context) which:
    * Builds systemPrompt from ego personality, training
    * Calls alterEgoAIService.generateAIResponse(systemPrompt, message, {history, ...context})
    * Increments ego.totalReplies
    * Updates ego.lastActive = new Date()
    * Prepends activity log entry: {action: context.action||'dm_reply', targetUser: context.targetUserId, originalMessage: message.substring(0,200), egoResponse: response.substring(0,200), timestamp: Date.now()}
    * Caps activityLog at 100 entries
    * save()
    * Returns AI response string
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {response: string}}
Response Status: 200; 400 if validation fails; 404 if not found; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: '...message required...'} for validation

## Handler: toggle
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - If toggling from false to true (activating):
    * Check ego.trainingLevel >= 1; if not, return 400 {success: false, message: 'Complete at least 1 training step to activate'}
  - Toggle ego.isActive = !ego.isActive
  - save()
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {isActive: boolean}}
Response Status: 200; 400 if validation fails; 404 if not found; 500 on error

## Handler: getStats
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Build response object from ego fields
  - Call getActiveProvider() from alterEgoAIService
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {totalReplies, trainingLevel, isActive, lastActive, personality, guessWhoStats: {totalGames, correctGuesses, fooledCount}, aiProvider: string}}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error

## Handler: learn
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: {trigger: string (required), response: string (required)}
Validation:
  - trigger: required, non-empty
  - response: required, non-empty
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - If trigger or response missing, return 400 {success: false, message: 'Trigger and response required'}
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Call ego.learnFromUser(trigger, response) which:
    * Search ego.responsePatterns for existing pattern with matching trigger
    * If found, increment existing.frequency
    * If not found, push {trigger, response, frequency: 1}
    * Sort patterns by frequency descending
    * Slice to top 100 patterns
    * save()
  - Return 200 {success: true, message: 'Learned!'}
Response Status: 200; 400 if validation fails; 404 if not found; 500 on error

## Handler: getActivityLog
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Parse page and limit from query
  - Calculate skip = (page - 1) * limit
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Slice activities = ego.activityLog.slice(skip, skip + limit)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: activities, pagination: {page, limit, total: ego.activityLog.length, hasMore: skip + limit < total}}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: [{action: enum, targetUser: ObjectId|null, originalMessage: string, egoResponse: string, wasRevealed?: boolean, guessedCorrectly?: boolean, timestamp: Date}]

## Handler: recordGuess
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: {guessedCorrectly: boolean (required)}
Validation:
  - guessedCorrectly: required, must be boolean
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - If typeof guessedCorrectly !== 'boolean', return 400
  - Query AlterEgo.findOne({user: userId})
  - If not found, return 404
  - Call ego.recordGuessResult(guessedCorrectly) which:
    * Increment ego.guessWhoStats.totalGames
    * If guessedCorrectly true, increment correctGuesses; else increment fooledCount
    * save()
    * Return guessWhoStats
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: stats} where stats = {totalGames, correctGuesses, fooledCount}
Response Status: 200; 400 if validation fails; 404 if not found; 500 on error

## Handler: getAIStatus
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Logic:
  - Call getActiveProvider() from alterEgoAIService → returns string provider name
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {provider: string, isAIEnabled: provider !== 'template'}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: error.message}

## Database Schema: AlterEgo
Collection: 'alterego'
Fields:
  user: ObjectId (ref User, unique index)
  name: string (maxlength 20, trim)
  personality: enum['friendly','funny','professional','mysterious','chill'] default='friendly'
  training: {howAreYou?: string, favoriteTopics?: [string], humorStyle?: string, complimentResponse?: string, hotTakes?: [string], phrases?: [string], emojis?: [string]}
  trainingLevel: number (0-5, default 0)
  responsePatterns: [{trigger: string, response: string, frequency: number}] (capped at 100)
  vocabulary: {commonWords: [string], slang: [string], greetings: [string], farewells: [string]}
  conversations: [{withUser: ObjectId, messages: [{role: enum['user','ego'], content: string, timestamp: Date}], lastActive: Date}]
  isActive: boolean (default false)
  autoReplyDM: boolean (default true)
  autoReplyComments: boolean (default false)
  activeHours: {start: number 0-23 default 0, end: number 0-24 default 24}
  totalReplies: number (default 0)
  satisfactionScore: number (default 0)
  lastActive: Date|null
  activityLog: [{action: enum['dm_reply','comment_reply','guess_game'], targetUser: ObjectId, originalMessage: string, egoResponse: string, wasRevealed: boolean, guessedCorrectly: boolean|null, timestamp: Date}] (capped at 100)
  guessWhoStats: {totalGames: number, correctGuesses: number, fooledCount: number}
  timestamps: true (createdAt, updatedAt)
Indexes: {user: 1}, {isActive: 1}

## Dependencies
- AlterEgo model (models/AlterEgo.js)
- alterEgoAIService.generateAIResponse(systemPrompt, message, context)
- alterEgoAIService.getActiveProvider() → string

## Cache/Redis Usage
None observed in controller

## Third-Party APIs
alteEgoAIService (internal, calls Gemini/OpenAI/fallback template)

files.purpose: Manages SocialDNA profiles — personality DNA vibes, evolution tracking, DNA matching/twins, and shareable cards
routes: GET /me — getMyDNA — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch or create user's SocialDNA with current strands
GET /share-card — getShareCard — [verifyAccessToken] — get formatted card data for sharing
POST /share — recordShare — [verifyAccessToken] — increment cardShareCount for viral tracking
GET /evolution — getEvolution — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch weekly snapshot evolution
GET /twins — findTwins — [verifyAccessToken] — find users with similar DNA (limit queryable)
GET /match/:targetUserId — getCompatibility — [verifyAccessToken] — calculate DNA match % with another user
GET /user/:userId — getUserDNA — [verifyAccessToken] — view another user's public DNA
files.details:
## Handler: getMyDNA
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Params: none
Query: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId from req.user.userId
  - Call SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userId) which:
    * Queries collection 'socialdna' filter {user: userId}
    * If not exists, create new doc with default strands {chill: 20, hype: 20, sad: 20, funny: 20, creative: 20}
    * save()
    * Returns document
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {strands, dominantVibe, totalSignals, streak, weeksTracked: totalWeeksTracked, latestInsights, lastComputedAt}}
Response Status: 200 on success; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, error: 'Failed to get DNA profile'}
DB Query: SocialDNA.findOne({user: userId}); SocialDNA.create({user})
Response Data Shape: {strands: {chill, hype, sad, funny, creative}, dominantVibe: string, totalSignals: number, streak: number, weeksTracked: number, latestInsights: [{type, message, metric, value, generatedAt}], lastComputedAt: Date}

## Handler: getShareCard
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Call SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userId)
  - Call dna.getShareCardData() which returns:
    * {strands: [{vibe, percentage, emoji, color}] (sorted by % desc), dominantVibe, dominantEmoji, dominantColor, streak, weeksTracked}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: cardData}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: {strands: [{vibe: string, percentage: number, emoji: string, color: string}], dominantVibe: string, dominantEmoji: string, dominantColor: string, streak: number, weeksTracked: number}

## Handler: recordShare
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Call SocialDNA.findOneAndUpdate({user: userId}, {$inc: {cardShareCount: 1}})
  - Return 200 {success: true, message: 'Share recorded'}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Query: SocialDNA.findOneAndUpdate({user: userId}, {$inc: {cardShareCount: 1}}, {new: false})
Side Effects: Increments cardShareCount by 1

## Handler: getEvolution
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {weeks?: number=12}
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Parse limit = parseInt(req.query.weeks) || 12
  - Call SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userId)
  - Extract last `limit` snapshots from dna.snapshots
  - Map each snapshot to {weekStart, weekEnd, strands, dominantVibe, insights, totalInteractions}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {snapshots: [...], currentStrands: dna.strands, totalWeeks: dna.totalWeeksTracked}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: {snapshots: [{weekStart: Date, weekEnd: Date, strands, dominantVibe, insights: [...], totalInteractions: number}], currentStrands: {...}, totalWeeks: number}

## Handler: getCompatibility
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {targetUserId: string (required)}
Body: none
Validation:
  - targetUserId: required in params
Logic:
  - Extract userId from req.user and targetUserId from params
  - If targetUserId missing, return 400 {success: false, error: 'Target user ID required'}
  - Call DNAMatchAlgo.getCompatibility(userId, targetUserId) which:
    * Fetches both users' SocialDNA
    * Calls sourceUser.matchWith(targetDNA) → returns match % (0-100)
    * Returns result object with matchPercent and details
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: result}
Response Status: 200; 400 if validation fails; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: {matchPercent: number, ...} (from DNAMatchAlgo)

## Handler: findTwins
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Parse limit = parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20
  - Call DNAMatchAlgo.findTwins(userId, limit) which:
    * Queries SocialDNA for users with similar strands (using matchWith algorithm)
    * Returns array of {user, matchPercent} sorted by matchPercent desc
    * Limited to `limit` results
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: twins}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: [{user: ObjectId|{_id, username, profile}, matchPercent: number}]

## Handler: getUserDNA
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {userId: string (required)}
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract targetUserId from params
  - Query SocialDNA.findOne({user: targetUserId})
  - If not found, return 200 {success: true, data: null, message: 'User has no DNA profile yet'}
  - Return public DNA only (no rawSignals): {strands, dominantVibe, weeksTracked: totalWeeksTracked, streak}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {...}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Note: Returns 200 even if data is null (no 404)
Response Data Shape: {strands: {...}, dominantVibe: string, weeksTracked: number, streak: number} or null

## Database Schema: SocialDNA
Collection: 'socialdna'
Fields:
  user: ObjectId (ref User, unique index)
  strands: {chill: number (default 20), hype: number (default 20), sad: number (default 20), funny: number (default 20), creative: number (default 20)}
  dominantVibe: enum['chill','hype','sad','funny','creative'] (default 'chill')
  rawSignals: {chill, hype, sad, funny, creative: number} (default 0)
  totalSignals: number (default 0)
  snapshots: [{weekStart: Date, weekEnd: Date, strands: {...}, dominantVibe: string, insights: [{type, message, metric, value}], totalInteractions: number}]
  twins: [{user: ObjectId, matchPercent: number, discoveredAt: Date}]
  latestInsights: [{type, message, metric, value, generatedAt: Date}]
  streak: number (default 0)
  totalWeeksTracked: number (default 0)
  cardShareCount: number (default 0)
  lastComputedAt: Date (default now)
  timestamps: true
Indexes: {user: 1, unique}, {dominantVibe: 1}, {twins.user: 1}, {totalSignals: 1}

## Dependencies
- SocialDNA model (models/SocialDNA.js)
- DNAMatchAlgo (../Algorithms/DNAMatchAlgo)

## Cache/Redis Usage
None observed

## Side Effects
- recordShare increments cardShareCount
- getEvolution may trigger snapshot computation via model methods

files.purpose: Manages gamified Pulse Score reputation system — score calculations, tiers, leaderboards, and achievements
routes: GET /me — getMyScore — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch user's Pulse Score with display data
GET /breakdown — getBreakdown — [verifyAccessToken] — detailed score breakdown with metrics, history, achievements
GET /leaderboard — getLeaderboard — [verifyAccessToken] — top N users by score with requester's rank
GET /user/:userId — getUserScore — [verifyAccessToken] — view another user's public score
GET /achievements — getAchievements — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch user's unlocked achievements
GET /history — getHistory — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch score history for chart plotting
files.details:
## Handler: getMyScore
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Query: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId from req.user.userId
  - Call PulseScore.getOrCreate(userId) which:
    * Queries collection 'pulsescore' filter {user: userId}
    * If not exists, create new doc with defaults (score: 0, tier: 'newcomer')
    * save()
    * Returns document
  - Call ps.getDisplayData() which returns:
    * {score, tier, tierLabel, tierEmoji, tierColor, components: {engagement, consistency, community, reach, creativity}, streak, achievements: count, nextTierAt, progressToNext}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: displayData}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, error: 'Failed to get Pulse Score'}
DB Query: PulseScore.findOne({user: userId}); PulseScore.create({user})
Response Data Shape: {score: number 0-1000, tier: string, tierLabel: string, tierEmoji: string, tierColor: string, components: {engagement, consistency, community, reach, creativity: number}, streak: number, achievements: number (count), nextTierAt: number|null, progressToNext: number 0-100}

## Handler: getBreakdown
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Call PulseScore.getOrCreate(userId)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {displayData..., metrics: ps.metrics, history: ps.history.slice(-30), achievements: ps.achievements}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: {...getDisplayData(), metrics: {totalPosts, totalLikesGiven, totalLikesReceived, totalCommentsGiven, totalCommentsReceived, totalFollowers, totalFollowing, totalShares, totalViews, uniqueVibes, mediaPostsCount, daysActive, currentStreak, longestStreak, lastActiveDate}, history: [{date: Date, score, tier, delta}] (last 30), achievements: [{id, name, description, emoji, unlockedAt}]}

## Handler: getLeaderboard
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {limit?: number=50}
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Parse limit = Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(req.query.limit) || 50, 1), 1000)
  - Call PulseScore.getLeaderboard(limit) which:
    * Query: find({score: {$gt: 0}}).sort({score: -1}).limit(limit).populate('user', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar isVerified').lean()
    * Returns array of docs
  - Call PulseScore.getUserRank(userId) which:
    * Fetch user's score
    * Count: countDocuments({score: {$gt: userScore.score}}) + 1 = rank
    * Count: countDocuments({score: {$gt: 0}}) = total
    * Calculate percentile = ((total - rank) / total) * 100
    * Return {rank, total, percentile}
  - Map leaderboard to [{rank: i+1, user: userId, score, tier}]
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {leaderboard: [...], myRank: {rank, total, percentile}}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: {leaderboard: [{rank: number, user: ObjectId, score: number, tier: string}], myRank: {rank: number, total: number, percentile: number}}
DB Query: PulseScore.find({score: {$gt: 0}}).sort({score: -1}).limit(limit).populate('user', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar isVerified'); PulseScore.countDocuments({score: {$gt: userScore}}) + 1

## Handler: getUserScore
Signature: async (req, res) => void
