Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {userId: string (required)}
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract targetUserId from params
  - Query PulseScore.findOne({user: targetUserId})
  - If not found, return 200 {success: true, data: {score: 0, tier: 'newcomer', tierEmoji: '🌱'}}
  - Call ps.getDisplayData()
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: displayData}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Note: Returns 200 with default data if user not found (no 404)
Response Data Shape: {score, tier, tierLabel, tierEmoji, tierColor, components, streak, achievements, nextTierAt, progressToNext}

## Handler: getAchievements
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Call PulseScore.getOrCreate(userId)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: ps.achievements}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: [{id: string, name: string, description: string, emoji: string, unlockedAt: Date}]

## Handler: getHistory
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {days?: number=30}
Logic:
  - Extract userId
  - Parse days = parseInt(req.query.days) || 30
  - Call PulseScore.getOrCreate(userId)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: ps.history.slice(-days)}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Response Data Shape: [{date: Date, score: number, tier: string, delta: number|null}]

## Database Schema: PulseScore
Collection: 'pulsescore'
Fields:
  user: ObjectId (ref User, unique index)
  score: number (0-1000, default 0)
  tier: enum['newcomer','rising','established','influencer','icon'] (default 'newcomer')
  components: {engagement: 0-200, consistency: 0-200, community: 0-200, reach: 0-200, creativity: 0-200}
  metrics: {totalPosts, totalLikesGiven, totalLikesReceived, totalCommentsGiven, totalCommentsReceived, totalFollowers, totalFollowing, totalShares, totalViews, uniqueVibes, mediaPostsCount, daysActive, currentStreak, longestStreak, lastActiveDate: YYYY-MM-DD string}
  history: [{date: Date, score, tier, delta: number}] (capped at 90 days)
  achievements: [{id, name, description, emoji, unlockedAt: Date}]
  lastComputedAt: Date
  timestamps: true
Indexes: {score: -1}, {tier: 1}, {user: 1, unique}

Tier Configuration:
  newcomer: 0-199 emoji='🌱' label='Newcomer' color='#8BC34A'
  rising: 200-399 emoji='⭐' label='Rising Star' color='#FFC107'
  established: 400-599 emoji='💫' label='Established' color='#FF9800'
  influencer: 600-799 emoji='🔥' label='Influencer' color='#F44336'
  icon: 800-1000 emoji='👑' label='Icon' color='#9C27B0'

## Achievements Earned By Conditions:
  first_post: totalPosts >= 1
  posts_10: totalPosts >= 10
  posts_50: totalPosts >= 50
  streak_3: currentStreak >= 3
  streak_7: currentStreak >= 7
  streak_30: currentStreak >= 30
  score_100: score >= 100
  score_500: score >= 500
  score_800: score >= 800
  likes_100: totalLikesReceived >= 100
  community_50: totalCommentsGiven >= 50
  followers_100: totalFollowers >= 100

## Dependencies
- PulseScore model (models/PulseScore.js)

## Cache/Redis Usage
None observed

## Side Effects
None (read-only handlers)

files.purpose: Manages time-limited Pulse Drops — viral content response challenges with participation, responses, and expiration
routes: GET / — getActive — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch active drops with time remaining
GET /:dropId — getById — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch single drop with populated relations
POST /:dropId/join — join — [verifyAccessToken] — join a drop (with optional response post)
POST /:dropId/respond — createResponse — [verifyAccessToken] — create response post and join drop
GET /:dropId/responses — getResponses — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch paginated responses to a drop
POST /create — createDrop — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — admin create manual drop
N/A — expireDrops() — cron job — expire old drops automatically
files.details:
## Handler: getActive
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - Parse limit = parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20
  - Call PulseDrop.getActiveDrops(limit) which:
    * Query: find({status: 'active', expiresAt: {$gt: now}}).sort({trending: -1, trendingScore: -1, participantCount: -1}).limit(limit).populate('triggerPost', 'content media author').populate('featuredResponses', 'content media stats')
    * Returns array
  - Map each drop: {...drop.toObject(), timeRemaining: drop.getTimeRemaining()}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: drops[]}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: error.message}
DB Query: PulseDrop.find({status: 'active', expiresAt: {$gt: Date.now()}}).sort({trending, trendingScore, participantCount}).limit(limit).populate(...)
Response Data Shape: [{_id, title, description, coverImage, status, participantCount, responseCount, expiresAt, timeRemaining: 'XhYm', triggerPost, featuredResponses, ...}]

## Handler: getById
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {dropId: string (required)}
Body: none
Logic:
  - Extract dropId from params
  - Query: PulseDrop.findById(dropId).populate('triggerPost', 'content media author stats').populate('featuredResponses', 'content media author stats').populate('participants.user', 'username profile.avatar')
  - If not found, return 404 {success: false, message: 'Drop not found'}
  - Call drop.getTimeRemaining() → 'XhYm' string
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {...drop.toObject(), timeRemaining}}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.findById(dropId).populate('triggerPost', 'content media author stats').populate('featuredResponses', 'content media author stats').populate('participants.user', 'username profile.avatar')
Response Data Shape: {_id, title, description, coverImage, triggerPost, participants: [{user, response, joinedAt}], participantCount, responseCount, expiresAt, status, timeRemaining, ...}

## Handler: join
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {dropId: string (required)}
Body: {responsePostId?: string|null}
Logic:
  - Extract dropId, userId from req.user.userId
  - Extract responsePostId from body (optional)
  - Query PulseDrop.findById(dropId)
  - If not found, return 404 {success: false, message: 'Drop not found'}
  - If drop.status !== 'active', return 400 {success: false, message: 'This drop has expired'}
  - Call drop.join(userId, responsePostId) which:
    * Check if user already in participants
    * If exists and responsePostId provided, update existing.response; increment responseCount
    * If new, push {user, response, joinedAt}; increment participantCount
    * If responsePostId provided, increment responseCount
    * Recalculate trendingScore = participantCount * 2 + responseCount * 5
    * save()
  - Return 201 {success: true, data: {participantCount, responseCount}}
Response Status: 201; 400 if expired; 404 if not found; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.findById(dropId); drop.save()
Side Effects: Increments participantCount, responseCount; updates trendingScore

## Handler: createResponse
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {dropId: string (required)}
Body: {content: string, media?: array}
Validation:
  - content: required (via Post.create)
  - media: optional
Logic:
  - Extract dropId, userId
  - Query PulseDrop.findById(dropId)
  - If not found or status !== 'active', return 404 {success: false, message: 'Active drop not found'}
  - Call Post.create({author: userId, content, media, type: 'pulse_drop_response', metadata: {pulseDropId: dropId}})
  - Call drop.join(userId, post._id)
  - Return 201 {success: true, data: post}
Response Status: 201; 404 if not found; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.findById(dropId); Post.create({...}); drop.save()
Side Effects: Creates Post, joins drop
Response Data Shape: Post document (full)

## Handler: getResponses
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Params: {dropId: string (required)}
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - Extract dropId
  - Parse page, limit
  - Query PulseDrop.findById(dropId)
  - If not found, return 404
  - Extract responseIds from drop.participants where participant.response exists
  - Query: Post.find({_id: {$in: responseIds}}).sort({'stats.likes': -1}).skip((page-1)*limit).limit(limit).populate('author', 'username profile.avatar')
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: responses[]}
Response Status: 200; 404 if drop not found; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.findById(dropId); Post.find({_id: {$in: responseIds}}).sort({'stats.likes': -1}).skip(...).limit(...).populate('author', 'username profile.avatar')
Response Data Shape: [Post documents]

## Handler: createDrop (Admin)
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Body: {title: string (required), description?: string, coverImage?: string, hashtags?: [string], durationHours?: number=24}
Validation:
  - title: required, maxlength 60 chars
Logic:
  - Extract fields from body
  - Call PulseDrop.create({title, description, coverImage, hashtags: hashtags||[], triggerType: 'manual', expiresAt: Date.now() + durationHours * 60 * 60 * 1000})
  - Return 201 {success: true, data: drop}
Response Status: 201; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.create({...})
Side Effects: Creates new PulseDrop document
Response Data Shape: PulseDrop document

## Handler: expireDrops (Cron Job)
Signature: async () => Promise<void>
Auth: none (internal cron)
Logic:
  - Call PulseDrop.expireOld() which:
    * Query: updateMany({status: 'active', expiresAt: {$lte: Date.now()}}, {$set: {status: 'expired'}})
    * Updates all expired active drops to 'expired'
  - Log success or error
Side Effects: Updates status of expired drops
DB Query: PulseDrop.updateMany({status: 'active', expiresAt: {$lte: now}}, {$set: {status: 'expired'}})

## Database Schema: PulseDrop
Collection: 'pulsedrop' (implied)
Fields:
  title: string (required, maxlength 60, trim)
  description: string (maxlength 200)
  coverImage: string|null
  triggerPost: ObjectId (ref Post)
  triggerType: enum['viral','trending_hashtag','event','manual'] (default 'viral')
  participants: [{user: ObjectId ref User, response: ObjectId ref Post, joinedAt: Date}]
  participantCount: number (default 0)
  responseCount: number (default 0)
  startsAt: Date (default now)
  expiresAt: Date (required, default now + 24h)
  status: enum['active','expired','featured'] (default 'active')
  totalEngagement: number (default 0)
  trending: boolean (default false)
  trendingScore: number (default 0)
  hashtags: [string]
  featuredResponses: [ObjectId ref Post]
  timestamps: true (createdAt, updatedAt)
Indexes: {status: 1, expiresAt: 1}, {trendingScore: -1}, {hashtags: 1}

## Dependencies
- PulseDrop model (models/PulseDrop.js)
- Post model (models/Post.js)

## Cache/Redis Usage
None observed

## Side Effects
- join: increments participantCount, responseCount, updates trendingScore
- createResponse: creates Post, joins drop
- expireDrops: updates drop status to 'expired'

files.purpose: Handles media uploads to Cloudinary — single and batch image/video uploads with transformations and optimization
routes: POST /upload — uploadMedia — [verifyAccessToken, uploadLimiter, uploadGuard, upload.single('file')] — upload single media file
POST /upload-multiple — uploadMultipleMedia — [verifyAccessToken, uploadLimiter, uploadGuard, upload.array('files', 5)] — upload up to 5 media files
files.details:
## Handler: uploadMedia
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Middleware: uploadLimiter (rate limit), uploadGuard (pre-buffer validation), multer.single('file')
Body: multipart/form-data with file field
Validation:
  - req.file must exist; if missing return 400
Logic:
  - Check req.file exists; if not, return 400 {success: false, message: 'No file uploaded'}
  - Create cloudinary uploadStream with options:
    * folder: 'pulse/posts'
    * resource_type: 'auto' (detects type)
    * transformation: [{width: 1080, crop: 'limit'}, {quality: 'auto:good'}]
  - Stream req.file.buffer to uploadStream
  - On success, return 200 {success: true, data: {url: secure_url, publicId: public_id, width, height, format, type: resource_type}}
  - On error, return 500 {success: false, message: 'Upload failed', error: error.message}
Response Status: 200 on success; 400 if no file; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Upload failed'|'No file uploaded'|'Server error', error?: string}

Cloudinary Configuration:
  - cloud_name: from config.get('media.cloudinary.cloudName')
  - api_key: from config.get('media.cloudinary.apiKey')
  - api_secret: from config.get('media.cloudinary.apiSecret')

Response Data Shape: {url: string (secure_url), publicId: string, width: number, height: number, format: string, type: string}

## Handler: uploadMultipleMedia
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Middleware: uploadLimiter, uploadGuard, multer.array('files', 5) — max 5 files
Body: multipart/form-data with files[] fields
Validation:
  - req.files must exist and not empty; if missing/empty, return 400
Logic:
  - Check req.files exists and length > 0; if not, return 400 {success: false, message: 'No files uploaded'}
  - For each file, create Promise:
    * Create cloudinary uploadStream with same options as single upload
    * Stream file.buffer to uploadStream
    * Resolve with {url, publicId, width, height, format, type}
    * On error, reject
  - Use Promise.all() to wait for all uploads
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: results[]}
  - Catch error: return 500 {success: false, message: 'Upload failed', error: error.message}
Response Status: 200 on success; 400 if no files; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Upload failed'|'No files uploaded', error?: string}

Response Data Shape: [{url: string, publicId: string, width: number, height: number, format: string, type: string}]

## Dependencies
- cloudinary (npm package v2 API)
- config (../config) for Cloudinary credentials
- multer (express middleware, req.file/req.files)
- stream (Node.js native)

## Cache/Redis Usage
None

## Third-Party APIs
Cloudinary Upload API: https://cloudinary.com/documentation/image_upload_api
  - upload_stream(options, callback)
  - Transformations applied: width limit 1080px, auto quality compression
  - Folder structure: pulse/posts/{random-filename}

## Environment Variables Required
- config.get('media.cloudinary.cloudName')
- config.get('media.cloudinary.apiKey')
- config.get('media.cloudinary.apiSecret')

## Side Effects
- Creates assets on Cloudinary CDN
- Rate limited by uploadLimiter middleware

files.purpose: Integrates Tenor API for GIF search, trending, and category discovery with optional fallback
routes: GET /search — searchGifs — [verifyAccessToken] — search GIFs by query
GET /categories — getCategories — [verifyAccessToken] — fetch available GIF categories
files.details:
## Configuration
Tenor API Setup:
  - Base URL: https://tenor.googleapis.com/v2
  - API Key: process.env.TENOR_API_KEY
  - Client Key: process.env.TENOR_CLIENT_KEY (default 'pulse_app')

All handlers check ensureConfigured(res) which:
  - Returns true if TENOR_API_KEY is set
  - Returns false and sends 503 {success: false, message: 'GIF service is not configured'} if not set

## Handler: searchGifs
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {q: string (required), limit?: number=20}
Validation:
  - q: required, non-empty after trim
  - If ensureConfigured fails, return 503
Logic:
  - Parse limit = parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20
  - Extract q = req.query.q
  - If !q || !q.trim(), return 400 {success: false, message: 'Search query is required'}
  - Call axios.get('https://tenor.googleapis.com/v2/search', {params: {q: q.trim(), key: TENOR_API_KEY, client_key: TENOR_CLIENT_KEY, limit, media_filter: 'gif,tinygif', contentfilter: 'medium'}})
  - Map results: response.data.results.map(gif => ({id, url: media_formats.gif.url||'', preview: tinygif.url||gif.url||'', width: dims[0]||200, height: dims[1]||200, description: content_description||''}))
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: gifs[], count: gifs.length}
Response Status: 200; 400 if no query; 503 if not configured; 500 on API error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Failed to search GIFs'}
DB Query: none
Response Data Shape: {success: boolean, data: [{id: string, url: string, preview: string, width: number, height: number, description: string}], count: number}

Tenor API Request:
  - Endpoint: GET /search
  - Params: q, key, client_key, limit, media_filter='gif,tinygif', contentfilter='medium'
  - Returns: {results: [{id, content_description, media_formats: {gif: {url, dims: [w, h]}, tinygif: {url}}}]}

## Handler: getTrendingGifs
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: {limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - If ensureConfigured fails, return 503
  - Parse limit = parseInt(req.query.limit) || 20
  - Call axios.get('https://tenor.googleapis.com/v2/featured', {params: {key, client_key, limit, media_filter, contentfilter}})
  - Map results same as searchGifs
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: gifs[], count: gifs.length}
Response Status: 200; 503 if not configured; 500 on API error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Failed to fetch trending GIFs'}
Response Data Shape: same as searchGifs

Tenor API Request:
  - Endpoint: GET /featured
  - Params: key, client_key, limit, media_filter, contentfilter
  - Returns: {results: [gif objects]}

## Handler: getCategories
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken (required)
Query: none
Logic:
  - If ensureConfigured fails, return 503
  - Call axios.get('https://tenor.googleapis.com/v2/categories', {params: {key, client_key}})
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: response.data.tags || []}
Response Status: 200; 503 if not configured; 500 on API error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Failed to fetch categories'}
Response Data Shape: {success: boolean, data: [string]} (category names)

Tenor API Request:
  - Endpoint: GET /categories
  - Params: key, client_key
  - Returns: {tags: [string]}

## Dependencies
- axios (HTTP client)
- process.env for TENOR_API_KEY, TENOR_CLIENT_KEY

## Cache/Redis Usage
None observed (could be optimized with Redis caching for trending/categories)

## Third-Party APIs
Tenor API v2:
  - Base: https://tenor.googleapis.com/v2
  - Endpoints: /search, /featured, /categories
  - Auth: API key in query params
  - Response: {results: [{id, content_description, media_formats}]}
  - Media formats: gif, tinygif, mp4, nano (subset returned based on media_filter)
  - Content filter: 'off', 'low', 'medium', 'high' (we use 'medium')

## Environment Variables Required
- TENOR_API_KEY (required)
- TENOR_CLIENT_KEY (optional, defaults to 'pulse_app')

## Error Handling
All handlers:
  - Log error with console.error('Tenor error:', error?.response?.data || error.message)
  - Return generic {success: false, message: 'Failed to...'}
  - Axios errors include response.data for debugging

files.purpose: Generates Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags for social sharing — SEO-optimized HTML pages with deep linking
routes: GET /post/:postId — sharePost — [public, no auth] — render OG page for post sharing
GET /profile/:username — shareProfile — [public, no auth] — render OG page for profile sharing
GET /reel/:reelId — shareReel — [public, no auth] — render OG page for reel sharing
files.details:
## Shared Helper Functions ### escapeHtml(str: string) => string Escapes HTML entities in strings for safe OG meta tag values: - & → & - < → < - > → > - " → " - Returns '' if input is falsy ### getServerUrl(req) => string Resolves public server URL from environment or request context: - Reads process.env.SERVER_URL if set - Falls back to: req.protocol://req.get('host') - Returns string like https://example.com or http://localhost:3000 ### renderOGPage({title, description, image, url, type='article', appDeepLink}) => string Generates complete HTML5 document with OG/Twitter meta tags and deep-link redirect script: - Creates with meta charset UTF-8, viewport - Sets og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type, og:site_name='Pulse' - Sets twitter:card='summary_large_image' with matching title/desc/image - Inlines CSS for fallback UI (dark theme, centered layout) - Renders

,

, elements with OG data - Includes inline JavaScript that: * Attempts deep link (appDeepLink) via window.location * Waits 1500ms for app to intercept * If app not opened (native app detection), redirects to https://getpulse.app - All text content escaped via escapeHtml() ## Handler: sharePost Signature: async (req, res) => void Auth: none (public route, no authentication required) Params: {postId: string (required)} Logic: - Extract postId from params - Query: Post.findById(postId).populate('author', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar').lean() - Validation: if !post || post.isActive === false || (post.visibility exists && !== 'public') * Return 404 res.status(404).send('Post not found') - Extract data: * authorName = post.isAnonymous ? 'Someone' : post.author?.profile?.displayName || post.author?.username || 'Someone' * text = typeof post.content === 'string' ? post.content : post.content?.text * description = text ? text.substring(0, 200) : 'Check out this post by {authorName} on Pulse' * image = post.content?.media?.[0]?.url || post.media?.[0]?.url || (post.isAnonymous ? '' : post.author?.profile?.avatar) || '' * serverUrl = getServerUrl(req) - Call renderOGPage({title: '{authorName} on Pulse', description, image, url: '{serverUrl}/share/post/{postId}', appDeepLink: 'pulse://post/{postId}'}) - Return: res.set('Content-Type', 'text/html').send(html) Response Status: 200 (HTML) on success; 404 if post not found or inactive Response Body: text/html (complete HTML5 document) DB Query: Post.findById(postId).populate('author', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar').lean() ## Handler: shareProfile Signature: async (req, res) => void Auth: none (public) Params: {username: string (required)} Logic: - Extract username from params - Query: User.findOne({username: username.toLowerCase(), isActive: true}).lean() - If !user, return 404 res.status(404).send('Profile not found') - Extract data: * displayName = user.profile?.displayName || user.username * isPrivate = !!user.privacy?.isPrivate * bio = (!isPrivate && user.profile?.bio) || 'Follow {displayName} on Pulse' * avatar = user.profile?.avatar || '' * serverUrl = getServerUrl(req) - Call renderOGPage({title: '{displayName} (@{username}) — Pulse', description: bio, image: avatar, url: '{serverUrl}/share/profile/{username}', type: 'profile', appDeepLink: 'pulse://profile/{username}'}) - Return HTML Response Status: 200 (HTML) on success; 404 if user not found or inactive DB Query: User.findOne({username: lowercase(username), isActive: true}).lean() Note: Privacy respected — hides bio for private accounts on public page ## Handler: shareReel Signature: async (req, res) => void Auth: none (public) Params: {reelId: string (required)} Logic: - Extract reelId from params - Query: Reel.findById(reelId).populate('author', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar').lean() - If !reel, return 404 res.status(404).send('Reel not found') - Extract data: * authorName = reel.author?.profile?.displayName || reel.author?.username || 'Someone' * description = reel.caption ? reel.caption.substring(0, 200) : 'Watch this reel by {authorName} on Pulse' * thumbnail = reel.thumbnailUrl || reel.author?.profile?.avatar || '' * serverUrl = getServerUrl(req) - Call renderOGPage({title: '{authorName}\'s Reel — Pulse', description, image: thumbnail, url: '{serverUrl}/share/reel/{reelId}', type: 'video.other', appDeepLink: 'pulse://reel/{reelId}'}) - Return HTML Response Status: 200 (HTML) on success; 404 if reel not found DB Query: Reel.findById(reelId).populate('author', 'username profile.displayName profile.avatar').lean() ## Database Queries - Post.findById(postId).populate('author', ...).lean() - User.findOne({username, isActive: true}).lean() - Reel.findById(reelId).populate('author', ...).lean() All use .lean() for read-only performance optimization (no Mongoose document overhead) ## HTTP Status Codes - 200: HTML document returned successfully - 404: Post/profile/reel not found or visibility violations - 500: Database or server error ## HTML Output Structure - Doctype: HTML5 - Meta tags: charset UTF-8, viewport responsive - Open Graph: og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type, og:site_name - Twitter Card: twitter:card='summary_large_image', twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image - Deep Link Script: attempts pulse:// URI, falls back to https://getpulse.app - Fallback UI: HTML form with button, centered dark theme ## Security Considerations - All user content escaped via escapeHtml() to prevent XSS - Only public posts and active users returned - Private account bios not exposed on public shares - Anonymous posts don't leak author info ## Third-Party Integrations - Social media crawlers (Facebook, Twitter, WhatsApp, etc.) scrape meta tags - Deep linking via pulse:// custom protocol (app-specific) - Fallback to https://getpulse.app landing page ## Dependencies - Post model (models/Post.js) - User model (models/User.js) - Reel model (models/Reel.js) ## Cache/Redis Usage None (page generated per-request, but crawlers cache responses)

files.purpose: Admin panel backend — user/post/reel management, moderation, dashboard stats, and drop administration
routes: POST /login — login — [authLimiter, public] — admin authentication with username/email
GET /stats — getStats — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — dashboard metrics and charts
GET /users — listUsers — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — paginated user list with filters
PATCH /users/:userId/role — updateUserRole — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — change user role (user/admin/moderator)
GET /posts — listPosts — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — paginated post moderation list
GET /reels — listReels — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — paginated reel list
DELETE /reels/:reelId — deleteReel — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — hard delete reel and likes
GET /drops — listDrops — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — paginated drop list
PATCH /drops/:dropId/expire — expireDrop — [verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin] — mark drop as expired
files.details:
## Handler: login
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: authLimiter (rate limit), public (no token required)
Body: {identifier: string (required), password: string (required)}
Validation:
  - identifier: required (username or email)
  - password: required, non-empty
Logic:
  - If !identifier || !password, return 400 {success: false, message: 'Identifier and password are required'}
  - Query: User.findOne({$or: [{username: identifier.toLowerCase().trim()}, {email: identifier.toLowerCase().trim()}], isActive: true}).select('+passwordHash')
  - If !user || !user.passwordHash, return 401 with generic error (don't reveal which field was wrong)
  - If user.role !== 'admin', return 401
  - Call bcrypt.compare(password, user.passwordHash)
  - If !matches, return 401
  - Call authService.createUserSession(user, {deviceId: 'admin-panel-{random}', platform: 'web', deviceName: 'Admin Panel'}, req.ip || '127.0.0.1')
    * Returns {tokens: {accessToken, refreshToken}}
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {tokens: {...}, user: {_id, username, email, role, avatar}}}
Response Status: 200 on success; 400 on validation error; 401 on auth failure; 500 on error
Error Response: {success: false, message: 'Invalid credentials'|'Login failed'}
DB Query: User.findOne({$or: [{username}, {email}], isActive: true}).select('+passwordHash')
Side Effects: Creates Session document, invalidates old sessions

## Handler: getStats
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Body: none
Logic:
  - Calculate since = Date.now() - 14 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000 (2 weeks ago)
  - Define dailyAgg(Model) which returns aggregation:
    * stage 1: $match {createdAt: {$gte: since}}
    * stage 2: $group {_id: {$dateToString: '%Y-%m-%d', date: createdAt}, count: {$sum: 1}}
    * stage 3: $sort {_id: 1}
  - Run in parallel with Promise.all():
    * User.countDocuments({})
    * User.countDocuments({isActive: true})
    * Post.countDocuments({})
    * Post.countDocuments({isActive: true})
    * Reel.countDocuments({})
    * Message.countDocuments({})
    * Conversation.countDocuments({})
    * Session.countDocuments({isActive: true, expiresAt: {$gt: Date.now()}})
    * PulseDrop.countDocuments({status: 'active'})
    * dailyAgg(User)
    * dailyAgg(Post)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: {totals: {users, activeUsers, bannedUsers: total-active, posts, activePosts, reels, messages, conversations, activeSessions, activeDrops}, charts: {signupsByDay: [{date, count}], postsByDay: [{date, count}]}}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Queries: 
  - User.countDocuments({}) × 2
  - Post.countDocuments({}) × 2
  - Reel.countDocuments({})
  - Message.countDocuments({})
  - Conversation.countDocuments({})
  - Session.countDocuments({isActive: true, expiresAt: {$gt: now}})
  - PulseDrop.countDocuments({status: 'active'})
  - User.aggregate([{$match}, {$group}, {$sort}])
  - Post.aggregate([{$match}, {$group}, {$sort}])
Response Data Shape: {totals: {users, activeUsers, bannedUsers, posts, activePosts, reels, messages, conversations, activeSessions, activeDrops}, charts: {signupsByDay: [{date: YYYY-MM-DD, count}], postsByDay: [{date, count}]}}

## Handler: listUsers
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20, q?: string, role?: enum['user','admin','moderator'], status?: enum['active','banned']}
Validation:
  - page: clamped to min 1
  - limit: clamped to min 1, max 100
  - q: optional search string
  - role: optional, must be one of enum
  - status: optional, 'active' or 'banned'
Logic:
  - Parse and validate page (min 1), limit (1-100)
  - Build query object:
    * If q: add $or [{username: regex}, {email: regex}, {profile.displayName: regex}] (case-insensitive, escaped)
    * If role: add {role}
    * If status: add {isActive: status === 'active'}
  - Run in parallel:
    * User.find(query).select('username email phone role isActive isVerified isOnline lastActive createdAt stats profile.displayName profile.avatar avatar').sort({createdAt: -1}).skip((page-1)*limit).limit(limit).lean()
    * User.countDocuments(query)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: users[], pagination: {page, limit, total, totalPages}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Query: User.find(query).select(...).sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean(); User.countDocuments(query)
Response Data Shape: {success, data: [User], pagination: {page, limit, total, totalPages}}

## Handler: updateUserStatus
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Params: {userId: string (required)}
Body: {isActive: boolean (required)}
Validation:
  - isActive: must be boolean
  - userId: cannot be self (req.user.userId)
Logic:
  - If typeof isActive !== 'boolean', return 400 {success: false, message: 'isActive (boolean) is required'}
  - If userId === req.user.userId, return 400 {success: false, message: 'You cannot change your own account status'}
  - Call User.findByIdAndUpdate(userId, {$set: {isActive}}, {new: true}).select('username role isActive')
  - If !user, return 404
  - If isActive = false (banning):
    * Call Session.updateMany({userId, isActive: true}, {$set: {isActive: false}})
    * Try to del cache key 'auth_user:{userId}' (no error if miss)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: user}
Response Status: 200; 400 on validation; 404 if user not found; 500 on error
DB Query: User.findByIdAndUpdate(userId, {$set: {isActive}}, {new: true}); Session.updateMany({userId, isActive: true}, {$set: {isActive: false}}); cacheService.del(...)
Side Effects: Marks sessions inactive, clears auth cache to enforce ban immediately

## Handler: updateUserRole
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Params: {userId: string (required)}
Body: {role: enum['user','admin','moderator'] (required)}
Validation:
  - role: must be one of enum values
  - userId: cannot be self
Logic:
  - If !['user','admin','moderator'].includes(role), return 400
  - If userId === req.user.userId, return 400 {success: false, message: 'You cannot change your own role'}
  - Call User.findByIdAndUpdate(userId, {$set: {role}}, {new: true}).select('username role isActive')
  - If !user, return 404
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: user}
Response Status: 200; 400 on validation; 404 if user not found; 500 on error
DB Query: User.findByIdAndUpdate(userId, {$set: {role}}, {new: true}).select(...)

## Handler: listPosts
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20, q?: string, status?: enum['active','removed']}
Logic:
  - Parse and validate page, limit
  - Build query:
    * If q: add {content.text: {$regex: escaped(q), $options: 'i'}}
    * If status: add {isActive: status === 'active'}
  - Run in parallel:
    * Post.find(query).select('content visibility isActive isAnonymous stats createdAt author').populate('author', 'username profile.avatar avatar').sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean()
    * Post.countDocuments(query)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: posts[], pagination: {...}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Query: Post.find(query).select(...).populate('author', ...).sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean(); Post.countDocuments(query)
Response Data Shape: {success, data: [Post], pagination: {page, limit, total, totalPages}}

## Handler: updatePostStatus
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Params: {postId: string (required)}
Body: {isActive: boolean (required)}
Validation:
  - isActive: must be boolean
Logic:
  - If typeof isActive !== 'boolean', return 400
  - Call Post.findByIdAndUpdate(postId, {$set: {isActive}}, {new: true}).select('isActive author content.text')
  - If !post, return 404
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: post}
Response Status: 200; 400 on validation; 404 if post not found; 500 on error
DB Query: Post.findByIdAndUpdate(postId, {$set: {isActive}}, {new: true}).select(...)

## Handler: listReels
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20, q?: string}
Logic:
  - Parse and validate page, limit
  - Build query:
    * If q: add {caption: {$regex: escaped(q), $options: 'i'}}
  - Run in parallel:
    * Reel.find(query).select('caption videoUrl stats duration hashtags createdAt user').populate('user', 'username profile.avatar avatar').sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean()
    * Reel.countDocuments(query)
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: reels[], pagination: {...}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Query: Reel.find(query).select(...).populate('user', ...).sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean(); Reel.countDocuments(query)

## Handler: deleteReel
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Params: {reelId: string (required)}
Body: none
Logic:
  - Call Reel.findByIdAndDelete(reelId)
  - If !reel, return 404 {success: false, message: 'Reel not found'}
  - Call Like.deleteMany({targetType: 'reel', targetId: reelId})
  - Return 200 {success: true, message: 'Reel deleted'}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error
DB Query: Reel.findByIdAndDelete(reelId); Like.deleteMany({targetType: 'reel', targetId: reelId})
Side Effects: Hard deletes reel and all associated likes

## Handler: listDrops
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Query: {page?: number=1, limit?: number=20}
Logic:
  - Parse and validate page, limit
  - Run in parallel:
    * PulseDrop.find({}).sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean()
    * PulseDrop.countDocuments({})
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: drops[], pagination: {...}}
Response Status: 200; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.find({}).sort({createdAt: -1}).skip(...).limit(...).lean(); PulseDrop.countDocuments({})

## Handler: expireDrop
Signature: async (req, res) => void
Auth: verifyAccessToken, requireAdmin (required)
Params: {dropId: string (required)}
Body: none
Logic:
  - Call PulseDrop.findByIdAndUpdate(dropId, {$set: {status: 'expired'}}, {new: true})
  - If !drop, return 404
  - Return 200 {success: true, data: drop}
Response Status: 200; 404 if not found; 500 on error
DB Query: PulseDrop.findByIdAndUpdate(dropId, {$set: {status: 'expired'}}, {new: true})

## Database Models Used
- User
- Post
- Reel
- Message
- Conversation
- Session
- PulseDrop
- Like

## Dependencies
- bcryptjs (password comparison)
- crypto (random token generation)
- User, Post, Reel, Like, Message, Conversation, Session, PulseDrop models
- authService.createUserSession(user, deviceInfo, ip) → {tokens: {accessToken, refreshToken}}
- cacheService.del(key) → Promise
- escapeRegex utility for safe regex escaping

## Cache/Redis Usage
- cacheService.del('auth_user:{userId}') called when banning user to force logout

## HTTP Status Codes
- 200: Success
- 400: Validation error (e.g., isActive not boolean)
- 401: Authentication failure
- 404: Resource not found
- 500: Server error

## Security Considerations
- Login error message generic ('Invalid credentials') — doesn't reveal if user/password wrong
- Admins cannot change their own status/role — prevents self-lockout
- Banned users' sessions immediately invalidated
- Auth cache cleared on ban
- All search queries use escapeRegex to prevent injection
- Pagination limits enforced (max 100 per page)

specs.overview: The Pulse backend exposes 25 route modules with approximately 150+ endpoints across authentication, user profiles, content creation (posts/reels/snaps), feeds, messaging, gamification (roulette, chains, whispers, pulse drops), notifications, and admin features. All authenticated routes verify access tokens; protected features use role-based middleware. Each route handler validates inputs, queries MongoDB collections (Post, User, Reel, Snap, Conversation, Notification, etc.), may cache results in Redis, and returns JSON responses in the {success, data, error, code} convention. Middleware stack: cors, helmet, compression, rate limits (global + route-specific), body parsing (256KB JSON limit), input sanitization, pagination clamping, request metadata (timestamp, requestId). Error handling: 4xx for validation/auth, 5xx scrubbed in production (no internal details). Sentry error tracking if SENTRY_DSN configured.

files.purpose: Authentication lifecycle: OTP-based login (email/phone), username creation, token refresh, logout, Firebase login, and username availability checks.
files.details: INITIATEAUTH: POST body {method: 'email'|'phone', identifier, deviceId, platform?, deviceName?, appVersion?}. Phone validated 10-digit Indian (+91), email via regex. DeviceId required. Returns {success: true, data: {sessionId, tempToken, identifier, method}, ...}. Errors: 400 MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS, INVALID_METHOD, MISSING_DEVICE_ID; 400 AUTH_INITIATION_FAILED. VERIFYOTP: POST body {identifier, otp, method, deviceId, platform?}. Returns {success: true, data: {tokens: {accessToken, refreshToken}, user: {...}}}. 401 INVALID_OTP if expired/wrong; 400 OTP_VERIFICATION_FAILED. CREATEUSERNAME: POST body {tempToken, username, password, deviceId, platform?}. Username 3-30 chars alphanumeric+underscore, password validated by authService. Returns {success, data: {tokens, user}}. 409 USERNAME_EXISTS; 400 INVALID_USERNAME, USERNAME_ALREADY_SET. REFRESHTOKEN: POST body {refreshToken: string}. Returns {success, data: {accessToken, refreshToken}}. 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN on expired. RESENDOTP: POST body {identifier, method}. Returns {success, message}. CHECKUSERNAME: GET ?username=string. Returns {success, available: boolean}. FIREBASELOGIN: POST body {idToken?, accessToken?, deviceId, platform?, deviceName?, email?, name?, picture?, googleId?}. Returns {success, data: {tokens, user}}. 401 TOKEN_EXPIRED, AUTH_FAILED. GETCURRENTUSER: GET (verifyAccessToken). Returns {success, data: user}. LOGOUT: POST body {deviceId}. Revokes token via Redis denylist. Returns {success, message}.

files.purpose: User profile management: CRUD, search, follow/unfollow, block/unblock, onboarding, avatar upload.
files.details: SEARCHUSERS: GET ?q=string. Prefix search via User.username index, fallback to $text index. Returns {success: true, data: [{_id, username, displayName, avatar, isVerified}]}. Limit 20, excludes self. GETCURRENTUSER: Returns user with auto-synced stats (followers, following, posts from Follow/Post collections). GETBYUSERNAME: Returns {success, data: {...user, isFollowing, isOwnProfile, stats}}. GETUSERPOSTS: ?page, ?limit (clamped). Hides isAnonymous from non-owners. Returns {success, data: [...posts], pagination}. CHANGEPASSWORD: PATCH {oldPassword, newPassword}. 401 on mismatch. DELETEACCOUNT: Soft-delete (isActive=false), cascades. UPLOADAVATAR: POST multipart. Cloudinary upload. Returns {success, data: {url, publicId, width, height}}. UPDATEPROFILE: PATCH {displayName?, bio?, profile?}. TOGGLEFOLLOW: POST. Returns {success, data: {isFollowing}}. BLOCKUSER: POST. GETFOLLOWERS/FOLLOWING: GET /?page&limit. Returns {success, data: [...users], pagination}. NOTE: /me routes MUST come before /:username to avoid swallow.

files.purpose: Post lifecycle: create, read, search, delete, like, comment, trending hashtags.
files.details: CREATEPOST: POST body {text, media?, location?, visibility?, allowComments?, isAnonymous?}. Creates Post, increments user.stats.posts, triggers DNAMatchAlgo/PulseScore signals (non-blocking). Returns 201 {success, data: post} masked if anonymous. GETPOST: Increments stats.views atomically. Checks visibility (404 if private/followers-only + unauthorized). Returns {success, data: {...post, isLiked, likesCount, isBookmarked}}. SEARCHPOSTS: ?q. Posts $text index. Returns ranked. GETMYPOSTS/GETUSERPOSTS: ?page, ?limit. Non-owner hides isAnonymous. Returns {success, data: [...], pagination}. UPDATEPOST: PATCH /:postId {text?, media?, visibility?}. Owner only. DELETEPOST: DELETE soft-deletes. TOGGLELIKE: POST /:postId/like. Like collection, updates stats.likes atomically. ADDCOMMENT: POST /:postId/comments {text, parentCommentId?}. Nested support. GETCOMMENTS: GET /:postId/comments/?page&limit&sort. TOGGLECOMMENTLIKE: Comment Like management.

files.purpose: Feed algorithms: personalized (ForYou), chronological (Following), global, home, trending, location-based (Nearby).
files.details: All endpoints: ?page (clamped 1-50), ?limit (clamped 1-50). Cache: FOLLOW_GRAPH_TTL=60s (followgraph:{userId}), CANDIDATE_TTL=30s, PAGE_TTL=20s. GETFORUOUFEED: AI-ranked via embeddings + engagement. Returns {success, data: [...posts], pagination}. GETFOLLOWINGFEED: Chronological from followed. GETGLOBALFEED: All public posts. GETHOMEFEED: Following + own. GETTRENDINGPOSTS: Velocity-ranked. GETNEARBYPOSTS: Geo-query. All return masked anonymous posts, isLiked per user. posts.stats.likes is authoritative (never recomputed via aggregation).

files.purpose: Direct messages and group chats: create/list conversations, get messages, mark read, delete message.
files.details: GETORCREATECVERSATION: POST body {targetUserId}. Finds direct conversation (type='direct', 2 participants). If missing, creates. Returns {success, data: {...conversation}}. GETCONVERSATIONS: GET /?page&limit. Sorted by lastMessageAt desc. Populates participants, admins, createdBy. SEARCHCONVERSATIONS: GET /search?q. GETCONVERSATIONDETAILS: GET /:conversationId. User must be participant (403 if not). GETMESSAGES: GET /:conversationId/messages?limit=50&before=ISO_DATE. 403 if not participant. Returns messages sorted desc, excludes isDeleted. MARKCONVERSATIONREAD: POST /:conversationId/read. Sets unreadCounts[userId]=0. DELETEMESSAGE: DELETE /messages/:messageId. Soft-deletes (isDeleted=true).

files.purpose: Group management: create, update, add/remove members, make/remove admins, leave group.
files.details: CREATEGROUP: POST {groupName, participants: [...], groupDescription?, groupAvatar?}. Requires 2+ (auto-adds creator). Initializes unreadCounts. Creates system Message. Returns 201. GETGROUPDETAILS: GET /:groupId. Checks user in participants. UPDATEGROUPINFO: PUT {groupName?, groupDescription?, groupAvatar?}. Admin only. DELETEGROUP: DELETE. Soft-delete. ADDGROUPMEMBERS: POST /:groupId/members {userIds: [...]}. Admin only. REMOVEGROUPMEMBER: DELETE /:groupId/members/:userId. Admin/self. LEAVEGROUP: POST /:groupId/leave. Self. MAKEADMIN: POST /:groupId/admins {userId}. REMOVEADMIN: DELETE /:groupId/admins/:userId.

files.purpose: Collaborative storytelling: create chains, submit segments, vote on segments, like.
files.details: GETCHAINS: GET /?genre&page&limit&status='active'|'all'. Sorted by likes, contributorCount desc. Excludes pendingSegments, voters. Adds lastSegment virtual. Returns {success, data: [...]}. GETBYID: Full chain with populated segments. CREATECHAIN: POST {title, starterContent, genre?, previewImage?, maxSegments?, requireVotes?}. Auto-adds creator to contributors. Returns 201. SUBMITCGMENT: POST /:chainId/segment {content, media?}. Creates pendingSegment. GETPENDING: GET /:chainId/pending. VOTESEGMENT: POST /:chainId/segment/:segmentId/vote {voteType: 'approve'|'reject'}. When votes >= requireVotes, promotes. LIKECHAIN: POST /:chainId/like. Returns {isLiked}.

files.purpose: Anonymous location-based messages: create, vote (up/down), reply, report.
files.details: GETNEARBY: GET /?lng&lat&radius=5&limit=50. 2dsphere geo-query. Returns {success, data: [...whispers with distance calc]}. NB author never exposed. CREATEWHISPER: POST {content, lng, lat, city?, region?}. Stores location Point. Content max 280. Author internal only. Returns 201 {success, data: {...no author}}. VOTE: POST /:whisperId/vote {voteType: 'up'|'down'}. REPLY: POST /:whisperId/reply {content}. REPORT: POST /:whisperId/report {reason}.

files.purpose: Notification management: fetch, mark read, delete, get unread count by type.
files.details: GETNOTIFICATIONS: GET /?page&limit&type&unreadOnly='true'. Returns {success, data: [...notifications], pagination}. GETUNREADCOUNT: GET /count. Returns {success, data: {like: int, comment: int, follow: int, ...}}. MARKALLASREAD: PATCH /read-all?type. Sets read=true. MARKASREAD: PATCH /:id/read. 404 if not found. DELETENOTIFICATION: DELETE /:id.

files.purpose: FCM push notification registration and status.
files.details: REGISTER: POST {token, deviceId, platform?}. Calls pushService.registerToken(). Returns 200 {success: true, message}. 400 if token/deviceId missing. UNREGISTER: DELETE {deviceId}. Returns 200. STATUS: GET. Returns {success, data: {enabled, registeredDevices, firebaseConfigured}}.

files.purpose: GIF search and browsing via Tenor API.
files.details: SEARCHGIFS: GET /?q&limit=20. Tenor API. Returns {success, data: [{id, url, preview, width, height, description}], count}. 400 if q empty; 503 if not configured. GETTRENDINGGIFS: GET /?limit=20. Tenor featured. GETCATEGORIES: GET /. Returns {success, data: [...]tags}.

files.purpose: Image upload to Cloudinary.
files.details: UPLOADMEDIA: POST multipart {file}. uploadGuard pre-screens by Content-Length. Cloudinary upload to pulse/posts. Returns {success: true, data: {url, publicId, width, height, format, type}}. 400 if no file. UPLOADMULTIPLEMEDIA: POST {files: [...] (max 5)}. Returns {success, data: [{...}, ...]}.

files.purpose: Short-form video: create, feed (foryou/following), like, view tracking, share, comments.
files.details: CREATEREEL: POST multipart {file, caption?, ...}. videoUpload enforces video MIME. Streams to Cloudinary pulse/reels. Records signals. Returns 201 {success, data: reel}. GETREELSFEED: GET /?type='foryou'|'following'. ForYou uses ReelAlgo ranking (embeddings). Following chronological. Caches candidates (30s). TOGGLELIKE: POST /:reelId/like. TRACKVIEW: POST /:reelId/view. SHAREREEL: POST /:reelId/share. ADDCOMMENT: POST /:reelId/comments {text, parentCommentId?}. GETCOMMENTS: GET /:reelId/comments/?sort='best'|'top'|'new'|'controversial'&page&limit. TOGGLECOMMENTLIKE: Comment likes.

files.purpose: Disappearing stories/snaps: create, view, react, get viewers, delete.
files.details: CREATESNAP: POST multipart {file, audience: 'story'|'direct', recipients?, caption?, durationMs?}. Direct requires recipients. Cloudinary pulse/snaps. Returns 201 {success, data: snap}. GETSTORYAIL: GET /rail. Story rings grouped by author (own + followed, 24h active). GETDIRECTINBOX: GET /direct. Direct snaps to user. VIEWSNAP: POST /:snapId/view {reaction?}. Increments viewCount, adds viewers (max 500 inline). REACTSNAP: POST /:snapId/react {reaction}. GETVIEWERS: GET /:snapId/viewers. DELETESNAP: DELETE /:snapId. Creator only.

files.purpose: Random chat roulette: join queue, check status, send message, decide (connect/pass), leave, history.
files.details: JOINQUEUE: POST. Calls Roulette.findMatch(). If matched, returns {success, data: {sessionId, status: 'matched', partner, icebreaker, chatDuration}}. Else joins queue, returns {status: 'waiting'}. CHECKSTATUS: GET. Finds session for user. Returns {status, partner, timeRemaining}. Auto-transitions to 'deciding' if time up. SENDMESSAGE: POST {message}. DECIDE: POST {decision: 'connect'|'pass'}. If both connect, creates conversation. LEAVE: POST. GETHISTORY: GET. Returns past chats.

files.purpose: Gamified polls/challenges: list active, join, respond, get responses. Admin: create drop.
files.details: GETACTIVE: GET /?limit=20. Active drops. Returns {success, data: [...drops with timeRemaining]}. GETBYID: GET /:dropId. Returns {success, data: {...drop, timeRemaining}}. JOIN: POST /:dropId/join {responsePostId?}. Returns {participantCount, responseCount}. 400 if expired. CREATERESPONSE: POST /:dropId/respond {content, media?}. Returns 201 {success, data: post}. GETRESPONSES: GET /:dropId/responses/?page&limit. CREATEDROP (admin): POST {triggerPost, question, options?, duration, ...}. Returns 201.

files.purpose: AI twin personality: get, update settings, train, generate responses, toggle active, learn from interactions.
files.details: GETMYEGO: GET /me. Returns {success, data: {...alterEgo}}. UPDATE: PUT {name?, personality?, isActive?, autoReplyDM?, autoReplyComments?}. TRAIN: POST /train {exampleMessage, exampleResponse, ...}. TOGGLE: POST /toggle. Flips isActive. GENERATERESPONSE: POST /generate {message, context?}. Calls alterEgoAIService. LEARN: POST /learn {data}. GETACTIVITYLOG: GET /activity. RECORDGUESS: POST /guess {guessedUserId, isCorrect}. GETAISTATUS: GET /ai-status. Returns {provider, isReady}.

files.purpose: Social personality profile: get DNA, share card, evolution (weekly snapshots), find twins, compatibility.
files.details: GETMYDNA: GET /me. Returns {success, data: {strands, dominantVibe, totalSignals, streak, weeksTracked, latestInsights, lastComputedAt}}. GETSHARCARD: GET /share-card. Via getShareCardData(). RECORDSHARE: POST /share. Increments cardShareCount. GETEVOLUTION: GET /evolution?weeks=12. Returns snapshots. FINDTWINS: GET /twins. Returns {success, data: [{user, similarity, matchPercent}]}. GETCOMPATIBILITY: GET /match/:targetUserId. Returns {success, data: {score, strengths, challenges}}. GETUSERDNA: GET /user/:userId. Public view.

files.purpose: Gamification score: my score, breakdown, achievements, history, leaderboard.
files.details: GETMYSCORE: GET /me. Returns {success, data: {score, tier, nextMilestone, rank, ...}}. GETBREAKDOWN: GET /breakdown. Returns {...displayData, metrics, history (last 30), achievements}. GETACHIEVEMENTS: GET /achievements. Returns [{name, description, earnedAt, icon}]. GETHISTORY: GET /history. Returns [{timestamp, score}]. GETLEADERBOARD: GET /?limit=50. Returns {leaderboard: [{rank, user, score, tier}], myRank}. GETUSERSCORE: GET /user/:userId. Public view. Returns {score, tier, rank} or {score: 0}.

files.purpose: Save posts/reels for later: toggle bookmark, list bookmarks, check if bookmarked.
files.details: TOGGLEBOOKMARK: POST {itemId, itemType: 'post'|'reel'}. If exists, deletes, returns {success, data: {isBookmarked: false}}. Else creates, returns 201 {success, data: {isBookmarked: true}}. Increments Reel.stats.saves. GETBOOKMARKS: GET /?type&page&limit. Returns {success, data: [...items], pagination}. Adds isLiked/isSaved flags, maintains order. CHECKBOOKMARK: GET /check/:itemId. Returns {success, data: {isBookmarked: bool}}.

files.purpose: Referral codes and tracking: get/generate code, apply code, get referral stats.
files.details: GETMYCODE: GET /my-code. Generates 8-char cuid2 uppercase if missing. Returns {success, data: {referralCode, shareUrl, shareMessage, referralCount}}. APPLYCODE: POST /apply {code}. Validates code exists, checks !user.referredBy, links currentUser.referredBy=referrerId, awards 'early-adopter' badge to both. Increments referrer.referralCount. 409 if already referred; 404 if invalid. GETSTATS: GET /stats. Returns {success, data: {referralCode, totalReferrals, referredUsers: [{username, displayName, avatar, joinedAt}] (most recent 50)}}.

files.purpose: Admin dashboard: stats, user/post/reel/drop management, status updates, role changes.
files.details: LOGIN: POST /login {identifier, password}. Public (authLimiter). Queries User by username/email. Validates passwordHash, checks role='admin'. Returns {success, data: {tokens, user: {_id, username, email, role, avatar}}}. 401 'Invalid credentials' for all failures. GETSTATS: GET /stats. Daily aggregations (14 days). LISTUSERS: GET /?page&limit&search&status. UPDATEUSERSTATUS: PATCH /:userId/status {status: 'active'|'suspended'}. UPDATEUSERROLE: PATCH /:userId/role {role}. LISTPOSTS: GET /?page&limit. UPDATEPOSTSTATUS: PATCH /:postId/status {status}. LISTREELS: GET /?page&limit. DELETEREEL: DELETE /:reelId. Hard-delete. LISTDROPS: GET /?page&limit&status. CREATEDROP: POST (redirects to pulseDropController). EXIREDROP: PATCH /:dropId/expire. Sets status='expired'.

files.purpose: Open Graph share pages (public, no auth): post/profile/reel share cards for social previews.
files.details: SHAREPOST: GET /share/post/:postId. Queries Post (isActive=true, visibility='public'). Returns HTML with OG/Twitter meta tags. Anonymous posts use 'Someone' as author. Deep-link pulse://post/:postId with fallback. 404 if inactive/private. Returns text/html. SHAREPROFILE: GET /share/profile/:username. Queries User (isActive=true). Returns OG HTML with displayName, bio (hidden if private), avatar, deep-link pulse://profile/:username. SHAREREEL: GET /share/reel/:reelId. Returns og:type='video.other', thumbnail, caption, deep-link pulse://reel/:reelId. All sanitize HTML (escapeHtml XSS prevention). SERVER_URL env sets canonical base; falls back to request host.

specs.overview: The middleware subsystem provides authentication/authorization, rate limiting, upload handling with memory budgeting, and XSS sanitization for the Pulse backend. Auth middleware uses JWT tokens with Redis-backed revocation denylist and single-flighted user status caching to prevent thundering herd. Rate limiters implement per-user keying for authenticated traffic (handling CGNAT scenarios) with Redis coordination across cluster workers. Upload middleware enforces per-process in-flight byte accounting to prevent OOM. All middlewares follow Express conventions and respond with {success, error, code} JSON on rejection.

files.purpose: Authentication and authorization middleware for protecting routes and validating JWT access tokens. Implements single-flighted user status caching with jitter to prevent thundering herd on auth check failures. Handles token revocation denylist, user active status validation, and role-based access control.
files.details:
VERIFYACCESSTOKEN middleware:
- Signature: async (req, res, next) → void
- Extracts JWT from Authorization header (Bearer scheme only). If missing, returns 401 { success: false, error: 'Access token required', code: 'MISSING_ACCESS_TOKEN' }
- Calls jwtService.verifyAccessToken(token) which throws on expiry/invalid/wrong-type
- On error: intercepts error.message containing 'expired' → TOKEN_EXPIRED, 'Invalid' → INVALID_TOKEN, else AUTH_FAILED (all 401)
- Checks token revocation via redis key `revoked_token:{SHA256_HASH_OF_TOKEN}` (denylist; entries expire with JWT)
  - Hash: crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex')
  - Revoked: 401 { success: false, error: 'Access token revoked', code: 'TOKEN_REVOKED' }
  - Redis down: silently ignored, token expires naturally at 15m
- Calls resolveUserActive(userId) single-flight check (prevents DB stampede):
  - Cache key: `auth_user:{userId}`
  - Cache hit (value === 'true' or true): returns boolean
  - Cache miss: checks _authInflight Map for in-flight promise (concurrent requests await same promise)
  - Cache miss + not in-flight: queries User.findById(userId).select('isActive').lean() (DB hit)
    - Returns boolean active = !!(user && user.isActive)
    - Caches result as string in redis: TTL = 300 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 60) (jitter prevents synchronized expiry)
    - Stores promise in _authInflight, finally block deletes entry
  - Redis write failure: silently continues
  - Returns false → 401 { success: false, error: 'User not found or inactive', code: 'USER_NOT_FOUND' }
- On success: attaches req.user = { userId, username, email, isVerified } (all from JWT payload)
- Response codes: 401, 500 (auth_failed)
- Error codes: MISSING_ACCESS_TOKEN, INVALID_TOKEN, TOKEN_EXPIRED, TOKEN_REVOKED, USER_NOT_FOUND, AUTH_FAILED

OPTIONALAUTH middleware:
- Signature: async (req, res, next) → void
- No header or invalid Bearer: sets req.user = null, calls next()
- Extract token, call jwtService.verifyAccessToken(token)
- On any error: sets req.user = null, calls next() (silent fail, never sends response)
- On success: queries User.findById(decoded.userId) (full hydration, NOT lean)
  - Sets req.user = { userId, username, email, isVerified } if user exists and user.isActive === true
  - Sets req.user = null if user not found or inactive
- Always calls next()

REQUIREVERIFIED middleware:
- Signature: (req, res, next) → void (NOT async)
- Checks req.user exists and req.user.isVerified === true
- Missing auth: 401 { success: false, error: 'Authentication required', code: 'AUTH_REQUIRED' }
- Not verified: 403 { success: false, error: 'Account verification required', code: 'VERIFICATION_REQUIRED' }
- Otherwise: calls next()

REQUIREADMIN middleware:
- Signature: async (req, res, next) → void
- Missing auth: 401 { success: false, error: 'Authentication required', code: 'AUTH_REQUIRED' }
- Queries User.findById(req.user.userId) (full hydration)
  - Checks user exists and user.role === 'admin' (string)
  - Not admin: 403 { success: false, error: 'Admin access required', code: 'ADMIN_REQUIRED' }
  - DB error: 500 { success: false, error: 'Authorization check failed', code: 'AUTH_CHECK_FAILED' }
- On success: calls next()

INTERNAL STATE:
- _authInflight: Map<userId, Promise<boolean>> (per-process, prevents concurrent DB queries for same user)
- hashToken: crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex')
- resolveUserActive(userId): Returns Promise<boolean>; caches in redis with jitter, single-flights DB queries

files.purpose: Rate limiting middleware using express-rate-limit with Redis backend. Implements multiple limiters with different windows and maximums for various endpoint classes (global, auth, OTP, token refresh, uploads). Handles CGNAT scenarios where many users share one IP by keying authenticated requests by userId instead of IP.
files.details:
KEYBYUSEROIP function:
- Input: req (Express request)
- Returns: `u:{userId}` if req.user && req.user.userId exists, else `ip:{normalized_ip}` (ipKeyGenerator normalizes IPv6 to /64)
- Purpose: per-user rate limiting for authenticated requests (avoids CGNAT blocking entire carrier IP)

SKIPHEALTH function:
- Returns true if req.path in ['/health', '/health/ready', '/health/detailed', '/status'], else false

GLOBAL LIMITER:
- Window: 15min (900000ms)
- Max: parseInt(process.env.RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) || 1000 requests/window
- Key: keyByUserOrIp (user-based if authenticated, IP-based if anonymous)
- Redis prefix: 'rl:global:'
- Skip: skipHealth(req)
- Exceeded: 429 { success: false, error: 'Too many requests. Please slow down and try again later.', code: 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }
- Headers: standardHeaders: true (RateLimit-Limit, RateLimit-Remaining, RateLimit-Reset)

AUTH LIMITER:
- Window: 15min
- Max: parseInt(process.env.AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_MAX) || 100 requests/window/IP
- Key: ipKeyGenerator(req.ip) only (pre-auth, no user available)
- Redis prefix: 'rl:auth:'
- Exceeded: 429 { success: false, error: 'Too many authentication attempts. Please try again later.', code: 'AUTH_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }
- Note: per-credential brute-force protection is User model (loginAttempts/lockUntil), not this limiter

OTP LIMITER:
- Window: 15min
- Max: parseInt(process.env.OTP_RATE_LIMIT_MAX) || 30 requests/window/IP
- Key: ipKeyGenerator(req.ip) only
- Redis prefix: 'rl:otp:'
- Exceeded: 429 { success: false, error: 'Too many OTP requests, please try again later', code: 'OTP_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }
- Note: per-identifier (phone/email) limits are in customOTPService.checkRateLimit

REFRESH LIMITER:
- Window: 15min
- Max: 100 requests/window (hardcoded, no env var)
- Key: ipKeyGenerator(req.ip) only
- Redis prefix: 'rl:refresh:'
- Exceeded: 429 { success: false, error: 'Too many token refresh attempts. Please try again later.', code: 'REFRESH_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }

UPLOAD LIMITER:
- Window: 15min
- Max: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_RATE_LIMIT_MAX) || 60 requests/window
- Key: keyByUserOrIp (authenticated, user-based)
- Redis prefix: 'rl:upload:'
- Exceeded: 429 { success: false, error: 'Too many uploads. Please try again later.', code: 'UPLOAD_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED' }
- Note: counts upload requests only, not bytes (byte limits in uploadGuard)

Redis Store Configuration:
- RedisStore from rate-limit-redis
- sendCommand: (...args) => cacheService.redis.call(...args) (ioredis command)
- prefix: varies per limiter (counter keys: prefix + key_generator_output)
- Counters are atomic per Redis, correct across cluster workers
- passOnStoreError: true (fail open on Redis down for availability)

files.purpose: Multer-based file upload middleware with memory buffering, size limits, and per-process in-flight byte accounting to prevent OOM. Provides multiple upload configurations: standard images/videos, dedicated video reels with larger caps, and a byte-budget enforcement middleware (uploadGuard).
files.details:
UPLOAD (default multer instance):
- Storage: memoryStorage() (RAM buffer before Cloudinary upload)
- File filter: checks /jpeg|jpg|png|gif|mp4|mov|webp/ against both MIME type AND file extension (case-insensitive)
  - Both must match; on reject: cb(Error('Only images and videos allowed'))
- Limits:
  - fileSize: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_MAX_MB) || 25, converted to bytes
  - files: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_MAX_FILES) || 5
- Routes: POST /upload (single), POST /upload-multiple (up to 5), POST /users/me/avatar (single)

VIDEOUPLOAD (video-only multer instance):
- Storage: memoryStorage()
- File filter: checks /mp4|mov|webm|quicktime/ against MIME type OR file extension
  - cb(error || null, pass_boolean)
- Limits:
  - fileSize: parseInt(process.env.REEL_MAX_MB) || 80, converted to bytes
  - files: 1 (single only)
- Routes: POST /reels/create, POST /snaps/create

MAKEUPLOADGUARD(maxRequestMb) middleware factory:
- Returns: function(req, res, next) middleware
- Runs before multer to reject oversized requests early
- maxRequestBytes = (maxRequestMb || (MAX_FILE_MB * MAX_FILES + 2)) * 1024 * 1024
- Logic:
  - declared = parseInt(req.headers['content-length']) || 0
  - declared > maxRequestBytes: return 413 { success: false, error: 'Upload too large.', code: 'PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE' }
  - declared > 0 && inflightBytes + declared > MAX_INFLIGHT_BYTES (256MB default): return 503 { success: false, error: 'Server is busy handling uploads. Please retry in a moment.', code: 'UPLOAD_CAPACITY' }
  - Otherwise: inflightBytes += declared
  - Setup cleanup: release() decrements inflightBytes (Math.max(0, ...)), runs once on res.finish, res.close, or req.aborted
  - Call next()

UPLOADGUARD (instance):
- makeUploadGuard() with no args
- maxRequestBytes = (25 * 5 + 2) * 1024 * 1024 = 129MB default per request
- Shared inflightBytes counter (process-wide semaphore)

REELGUARD (instance):
- makeUploadGuard(REEL_MAX_MB + 2) = makeUploadGuard(82)
- maxRequestBytes = 82 * 1024 * 1024 = 85.9MB
- Accounts for multipart form overhead
- Shared inflightBytes counter with uploadGuard

PER-PROCESS IN-FLIGHT BYTE SEMAPHORE:
- let inflightBytes = 0 (module-scoped, not distributed)
- Incremented by Content-Length on each request
- Decremented on finish/close/abort
- Prevents N concurrent uploads from exceeding total memory budget

LIMITS EXPORT:
- MAX_FILE_MB: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_MAX_MB) || 25
- MAX_FILES: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_MAX_FILES) || 5
- MAX_INFLIGHT_MB: parseInt(process.env.UPLOAD_MAX_INFLIGHT_MB) || 256 (total per-process budget)
- REEL_MAX_MB: parseInt(process.env.REEL_MAX_MB) || 80

NOTES:
- Memory buffering: all files buffered in RAM (no streaming to disk)
- Content-Length header required for budget logic; missing treated as 0
- Declared size is proxy, not verified (client controls value)
- Per-process only: cluster workers each have separate inflightBytes counter
- File filter errors surface to handler as multer error or req.file.error

files.purpose: XSS prevention middleware that recursively sanitizes all string values in req.body using the xss library. Includes depth and node budget limits to prevent ReDoS/event loop blocking on pathological payloads.
files.details: SANITIZEBODY middleware: - Signature: (req, res, next) → void (NOT async) - If req.body exists and typeof req.body === 'object': req.body = deepSanitize(req.body, 0, { count: 0 }) - Always calls next() (never sends response) DEEPSANITIZE recursive function: - Signature: (obj, depth = 0, budget = { count: 0 }) → object - Budget: object with count property (passed by reference, shared across recursion) - Termination (return obj as-is): - depth > parseInt(process.env.SANITIZE_MAX_DEPTH) || 12: stops deeper recursion - budget.count > parseInt(process.env.SANITIZE_MAX_NODES) || 5000: stops processing - Each call: budget.count++ - Type-based logic: - typeof obj === 'string': - If obj.length > parseInt(process.env.SANITIZE_MAX_STRING) || 50000: return obj.slice(0, MAX_STRING_LEN) (truncate, skip xss) - Else: return xss(obj) (synchronous, sanitizes HTML/script tags) - Array.isArray(obj): return obj.map(item => deepSanitize(item, depth + 1, budget)) - obj !== null && typeof obj === 'object': - Iterate Object.keys(obj) - For each key: if budget.count > MAX_NODES: cleaned[key] = obj[key] (hard stop, no processing) - Else: cleaned[key] = deepSanitize(obj[key], depth + 1, budget) - Return cleaned object - Else: return obj unchanged (primitives: number, boolean, null, undefined) - Preserves structure; only transforms string values - Budget cumulative per entire body (not per branch) XSS LIBRARY: - xss(string) → string: removes/escapes dangerous HTML/JavaScript - Synchronous (linear cost to string length) - Default config: safe for HTML content ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES: - SANITIZE_MAX_DEPTH: max recursion depth (default 12) - SANITIZE_MAX_NODES: max total nodes processed (default 5000) - SANITIZE_MAX_STRING: max string length before truncation (default 50000) PATHOLOGICAL PAYLOAD DEFENSE: - Deeply-nested JSON stops at depth 12 - Wide JSON stops after 5000 keys total - Very long strings (>50000) truncated (xss cost linear) EXAMPLE: Input: { name: '', tags: ['xss', ''] } Output: { name: (sanitized), tags: [(sanitized), (sanitized)] }
