specs.overview: The config subsystem manages all environment configuration, service initialization, and system defaults for the Pulse backend. It provides centralized access to MongoDB, Redis, Firebase, SMTP, and feature flags. The Config class is a singleton that validates required env vars at startup, loads configuration from environment variables with sensible defaults, and exposes utility methods for environment checking and database URI selection based on NODE_ENV. DatabaseConfig handles MongoDB connection lifecycle with cluster-aware pool sizing. FirebaseConfig initializes Firebase Admin SDK for authentication. SMTPConfig manages email delivery via Brevo. RedisClient provides a singleton Redis connection instance. SwaggerConfig defines OpenAPI 3.0.0 documentation schema.

files.purpose: Central configuration loader. Validates required env vars at startup (JWT_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, TEMP_JWT_SECRET always required; MONGO_URI and SESSION_SECRET required in production). Loads and organizes all config into a nested object structure with sensible defaults. Provides get(path) method for accessing nested config values via dot notation.
exports: validateRequiredEnvVars(): void (throws process.exit(1) in production on missing critical vars)
loadConfig(): {server, database, jwt, security, otp, firebase, redis, media, rateLimit, cors, monitoring, features, debug}
printSummary(): void (logs config summary, masks mongo URI password)
files.details: SINGLETON PATTERN: module.exports = new Config() — instantiated on require. VALIDATION: On construction, validateRequiredEnvVars() checks process.env for: (1) always: JWT_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, TEMP_JWT_SECRET; (2) production-only: MONGO_URI, SESSION_SECRET, REDIS_URL or REDIS_HOST. Missing production vars call process.exit(1); dev mode logs warning and continues. loadConfig() reads from process.env with fallbacks: Server config: PORT (default 3000), NODE_ENV (default 'development'), API_VERSION (default 'v1'), SERVER_URL (default 'http://localhost:3000'), FRONTEND_URL (default 'http://localhost:3000'). Database config: MONGO_URI (default 'mongodb://localhost:27017/pulse'), MONGO_TEST_URI (default 'mongodb://localhost:27017/pulse_test'), pool sizing (maxPoolSize default 50, minPoolSize default 5, serverSelectionTimeoutMS default 5000 — all parsed as parseInt). JWT config: JWT_EXPIRES_IN (default '15m'), JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_IN (default '7d'). Security: BCRYPT_SALT_ROUNDS (default 12, parseInt). OTP config: YOUR_EMAIL, YOUR_EMAIL_APP_PASSWORD, YOUR_EMAIL_DISPLAY_NAME (default 'Pulse App'), YOUR_PHONE_NUMBER, YOUR_SMS_GATEWAY_URL, YOUR_SMS_API_KEY, YOUR_SMS_API_SECRET. Firebase: FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID, FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY_ID, FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY (with \n replacement: privateKey?.replace(/\\n/g, '\n')), FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL, FIREBASE_CLIENT_ID, FIREBASE_AUTH_URI, FIREBASE_TOKEN_URI. Redis: REDIS_URL (null fallback) OR REDIS_HOST (default 'localhost'), REDIS_PORT (default 6379, parseInt), REDIS_PASSWORD (null fallback), REDIS_MAX_RETRIES (default 3, parseInt), REDIS_RETRY_DELAY_ON_FAILOVER (default 100, parseInt). Media: Cloudinary (CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME, CLOUDINARY_API_KEY, CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET, CLOUDINARY_UPLOAD_PRESET, CLOUDINARY_FOLDER default 'pulse/media'), AWS S3 (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION default 'us-east-1', AWS_S3_BUCKET, AWS_S3_PUBLIC_BUCKET). Rate Limiting: RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS (default 900000 = 15min), RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS (default 100), RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_IP (default 1000). CORS: CORS_ORIGIN (split by comma, default ['http://localhost:3000']), CORS_CREDENTIALS (default false, parsed as === 'true'). Monitoring: SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT (default NODE_ENV), AMPLITUDE_API_KEY, MIXPANEL_TOKEN. Features (all boolean flags, === 'true'): ENABLE_FIREBASE_AUTH, ENABLE_GOOGLE_LOGIN, ENABLE_PHONE_VERIFICATION, ENABLE_EMAIL_VERIFICATION, ENABLE_TWO_FACTOR_AUTH, ENABLE_BACKGROUND_JOBS. Debug: LOG_LEVEL (default 'info'), ENABLE_REQUEST_LOGGING, ENABLE_API_DOCS, DEBUG_MODE (all boolean). get(path) splits by '.' and uses reduce to navigate nested structure: e.g., get('database.mongoUri') returns this.config.database.mongoUri. getDatabaseUri() returns this.config.database.mongoTestUri if isTest(), else this.config.database.mongoUri. printSummary() logs emoji-prefixed config snapshot (NODE_ENV, SERVER_URL, DATABASE with masked password, REDIS host:port, email/sms/firebase/cloudinary status).
files.schema: Configuration object returned by loadConfig() — nested structure with keys: server {port: number, nodeEnv: 'development'|'production'|'test', apiVersion: string, serverUrl: string, frontendUrl: string}, database {mongoUri: string, mongoTestUri: string, options: {maxPoolSize: number, minPoolSize: number, serverSelectionTimeoutMS: number}}, jwt {secret: string (required), refreshSecret: string (required), tempSecret: string (required), expiresIn: string, refreshExpiresIn: string}, security {bcryptSaltRounds: number, sessionSecret: string}, otp {email?: string, emailAppPassword?: string, emailDisplayName: string, phone?: string, smsGatewayUrl?: string, smsApiKey?: string, smsApiSecret?: string}, firebase {projectId?: string, privateKeyId?: string, privateKey?: string, clientEmail?: string, clientId?: string, authUri?: string, tokenUri?: string}, redis {url: string|null, host: string, port: number, password: string|null, maxRetries: number, retryDelayOnFailover: number}, media {cloudinary: {cloudName?: string, apiKey?: string, apiSecret?: string, uploadPreset?: string, folder: string}, aws: {accessKeyId?: string, secretAccessKey?: string, region: string, s3Bucket?: string, s3PublicBucket?: string}}, rateLimit {windowMs: number, maxRequests: number, maxRequestsPerIp: number}, cors {origin: string[], credentials: boolean}, monitoring {sentryDsn?: string, sentryEnvironment: string, amplitudeApiKey?: string, mixpanelToken?: string}, features {enableFirebaseAuth: boolean, enableGoogleLogin: boolean, enablePhoneVerification: boolean, enableEmailVerification: boolean, enableTwoFactorAuth: boolean, enableBackgroundJobs: boolean}, debug {logLevel: string, enableRequestLogging: boolean, enableApiDocs: boolean, debugMode: boolean}

files.purpose: MongoDB connection lifecycle manager. Handles cluster-aware connection pool sizing by deriving per-worker pool from container-wide budget. Manages connection state, health checks, index creation, and connection statistics.
exports: async connect(): Promise<mongoose.Connection> (creates MongoDB connection with cluster-aware pool sizing)
async isHealthy(): Promise<boolean> (ping to verify connection)
getConnectionStats(): {connected: boolean, readyState: number, host: string, port: number, name: string, collections: string[], models: string[]} | null
async createIndexes(): Promise<void> (creates all schema-defined indexes)
files.details: SINGLETON PATTERN: module.exports = new DatabaseConfig(). connect(): Checks if already connected (returns cached connection if true). Reads MONGO_URI from env (fallback 'mongodb://localhost:27017/pulse'). Pool sizing algorithm: (1) workersPerContainer = CLUSTER_WORKERS || os.cpus().length || 1; (2) explicitPool = parseInt(MONGO_OPTIONS_MAX_POOL_SIZE); (3) containerPoolBudget = MONGO_CONTAINER_POOL_BUDGET || 50; (4) derivedPerWorker = Math.max(5, Math.floor(containerPoolBudget / workersPerContainer)); (5) maxPoolSize = explicitPool || derivedPerWorker. This prevents per-worker pools from multiplying connection count in cluster mode. Calls mongoose.connect(MONGO_URI, options) with: maxPoolSize (derived), minPoolSize: MONGO_OPTIONS_MIN_POOL_SIZE || Math.min(2, maxPoolSize), maxIdleTimeMS: 30000, serverSelectionTimeoutMS: MONGO_OPTIONS_SERVER_SELECTION_TIMEOUT_MS || 5000, socketTimeoutMS: 45000, connectTimeoutMS: 10000, heartbeatFrequencyMS: 10000, retryWrites: true, retryReads: true. Sets this.isConnected = true and this.connection to mongoose.connection. Attaches listeners: on 'error' (set isConnected = false), on 'disconnected' (set isConnected = false), on 'reconnected' (set isConnected = true). In production on error, process.exit(1); in dev throws error. disconnect(): Calls mongoose.connection.close() if connected, sets isConnected = false. isHealthy(): Returns false if !isConnected; otherwise calls mongoose.connection.db.admin().ping() and returns true on success, false on error. getConnectionStats(): Returns null if !isConnected; otherwise {connected: this.isConnected, readyState: mongoose.connection.readyState (0=disconnected, 1=connected, 2=connecting, 3=disconnecting), host, port, name, collections: Object.keys(mongoose.connection.collections), models: mongoose.modelNames()}. createIndexes(): Calls mongoose.modelNames() to get all registered models. For each model, calls await mongoose.model(name).createIndexes() (additive, never drops indexes). Catches per-model errors (logs 'Index build failed for {name}'). Then iterates mongoose.connection.collections to call await collection.listIndexes().toArray() and log count per collection. Logs success or error.
files.schema: No schema — manages connection state via mongoose library. Key state: isConnected (boolean), connection (mongoose.Connection or null). Pool config follows container-wide budget pattern.

files.purpose: Firebase Admin SDK initialization and token verification/management. Provides methods to verify ID tokens, create custom tokens, and fetch user records from Firebase.
exports: async verifyIdToken(idToken: string): Promise<{uid: string, email: string, emailVerified: boolean, name?: string, picture?: string, provider: string}>
async createCustomToken(userId: string | ObjectId, additionalClaims?: object): Promise<string>
async getUser(uid: string): Promise<{uid: string, email?: string, emailVerified: boolean, displayName?: string, photoURL?: string, disabled: boolean, metadata: object, providerData: object[]}>
files.details: SINGLETON PATTERN: module.exports = new FirebaseConfig(). initialize(): Checks if already initialized (logs and returns cached app). Calls config.get('firebase') to read: projectId, privateKeyId, privateKey, clientEmail, clientId, authUri (fallback 'https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth'), tokenUri (fallback 'https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token'). Returns null if projectId, privateKey, or clientEmail missing (logs warning). Constructs serviceAccount object: {type: 'service_account', project_id, private_key_id, private_key, client_email, client_id, auth_uri, token_uri, auth_provider_x509_cert_url: 'https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs', client_x509_cert_url: `https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/${encodeURIComponent(clientEmail)}`}. Checks if admin.apps.length === 0 (prevents re-initialization). Calls admin.initializeApp({credential: admin.credential.cert(serviceAccount), projectId}) or admin.app() if already initialized. Sets this.auth = admin.auth() and this.isInitialized = true. Catches errors: logs error, in production logs warning 'Continuing without Firebase', returns null. verifyIdToken(idToken): Throws 'Firebase not initialized' if !isInitialized || !auth. Calls this.auth.verifyIdToken(idToken) and returns {uid, email, emailVerified: decodedToken.email_verified, name, picture, provider: decodedToken.firebase.sign_in_provider}. Catches errors and throws 'Invalid Firebase token'. createCustomToken(userId, additionalClaims={}): Throws 'Firebase not initialized' if !isInitialized || !auth. Calls this.auth.createCustomToken(userId.toString(), additionalClaims) and returns the token string. Catches errors and throws 'Failed to create custom token'. getUser(uid): Throws 'Firebase not initialized' if !isInitialized || !auth. Calls this.auth.getUser(uid) and returns {uid, email, emailVerified, displayName, photoURL, disabled, metadata, providerData}. Catches errors and throws 'User not found in Firebase'. isAvailable(): Returns this.isInitialized && this.auth !== null. getApp(): Returns this.app (may be null). getAuth(): Returns this.auth (may be null).
files.schema: No schema. State: app (admin.app.App | null), auth (admin.auth.Auth | null), isInitialized (boolean). All methods async. Errors thrown: 'Firebase not initialized', 'Invalid Firebase token', 'Failed to create custom token', 'User not found in Firebase'.

files.purpose: SMTP email service configuration using Brevo HTTP transport. Provides sendMail wrapper with auto-initialization and error handling.
exports: async sendMail(mailOptions: {to: string, subject: string, html?: string, text?: string, from?: string}): Promise<{response: string}>
files.details: SINGLETON PATTERN: module.exports = new SMTPConfig(). initialize(): Reads EMAIL_API_KEY from process.env. If missing, logs warning and returns null. Creates nodemailer transporter with new BrevoTransport({apiKey}). Sets this.isConfigured = true and returns transporter. Catches errors: logs error, returns null. sendMail(mailOptions): Checks if !isConfigured || !transporter. If so, calls this.initialize() and throws 'Email service failed to initialize' if still not ready. Wraps this.transporter.sendMail(mailOptions, callback) in Promise. Callback signature: (err, info) => { if (err) { console.error('❌ Email send error:', err); reject(err); } else { console.log(`✅ Email sent successfully to ${mailOptions.to}`); resolve(info); }}. mailOptions shape: {to: string, subject: string, html?: string, text?: string, from?: string} (from is optional, Brevo uses configured default). getTransporter(): Returns this.transporter (may be null).
files.schema: No schema. State: transporter (nodemailer.Transporter | null), isConfigured (boolean). sendMail requires EMAIL_API_KEY in .env. Brevo HTTP transport (not SMTP protocol). Response: nodemailer sends {response: string} on success.

files.purpose: Redis connection singleton. Initializes ioredis client with automatic retry strategy. No initialization method — client is ready on require.
exports: redisClient (ioredis.Redis instance, singleton, ready on require)
files.details: Requires ioredis and calls config.get('redis'). Constructs client using one of two patterns: (1) If redisConfig.url exists (non-null): new Redis(redisConfig.url, {maxRetriesPerRequest: redisConfig.maxRetries, retryStrategy(times) { return Math.min(times * 50, 2000); }}). Retry strategy: exponential backoff, 50ms * times, capped at 2000ms. (2) If redisConfig.url is null: new Redis({host: redisConfig.host, port: redisConfig.port, password: redisConfig.password, maxRetriesPerRequest: redisConfig.maxRetries}). redisConfig properties: url (null or connection string), host (default 'localhost'), port (default 6379), password (null or string), maxRetries (default 3). Attaches event listeners: on 'connect' -> console.log '✅ Redis Connected'; on 'error' -> console.error '❌ Redis Error:'. module.exports = redisClient makes it a singleton — returned immediately on require, not a class.
files.schema: No schema. Exports: ioredis.Redis instance (singleton, ready immediately). Configuration: read from config.get('redis'). No methods exported directly — caller uses standard Redis client methods (GET, SET, DEL, etc.).

specs.overview: The Pulse Socket.IO realtime subsystem enables live chat messaging, presence tracking, and typing indicators. It uses token-bucket rate limiting per-socket (in-memory), Redis-backed presence (not MongoDB) for efficient connection handling at scale, and sender profile caching to avoid re-reading users on every message. All socket events support dual naming (underscored + hyphenated) for cross-version compatibility with older app builds in the wild. The architecture scopes presence notifications to conversation peers only (no global broadcasts) and implements atomic message writes with in-process payload construction to minimize database round-trips.

files.purpose: Core Socket.IO realtime chat handlers: message send/receive, reactions, typing, presence, message deletion with cross-version event aliasing and Redis-backed presence tracking scoped to conversation peers.
exports: module.exports(io, socket): void - Registers all socket event handlers
resolveSenderProfile(socket) -> Promise<Object> - 3-tier cached sender identity (socket, Redis, DB)
files.details:
SOCKET.IO EVENT HANDLERS:

1. JOIN_CONVERSATION: Payload {conversationId: ObjectId|string}. Auth: Conversation.findOne({_id, participants:socket.userId}).select('_id'). Action: socket.join(conversationId) if found, else emit {message:'Unauthorized access to conversation'}.

2. LEAVE_CONVERSATION: Payload {conversationId: string}. Action: socket.leave(conversationId).

3. SEND_MESSAGE: Rate 5/sec burst 10. Payload {conversationId, content, type:enum, media?, replyTo?}. Auth: getAuthorizedConversation. DB: Message.create({conversation, sender:socket.userId, content, type, media?, replyTo?}). Profile: resolveSenderProfile (Redis TTL 600s + socket cache). Reply: Message.findById(replyTo).select('content sender type media').populate('sender','username name avatar profile.avatar').lean() if replyTo valid. Preview: type-dependent text ('Photo', 'GIF', 'Sticker', 'Video' or content). Unread update: For each non-sender participant, incUpdate[unreadCounts.{userId}]=1. Fire-and-forget Conversation.updateOne({_id:conversationId}, {lastMessage, lastMessageContent:preview, lastMessageAt:now, lastMessageSender:userId, $inc:incUpdate}). Broadcast io.to(conversationId).emit(['new_message','new-message'], {_id, conversation, sender:{_id,username,name,avatar,profile:{avatar},isVerified}, content, type, media, replyTo?, reactions:{}, readBy:[], isDeleted:false, createdAt, updatedAt}). Callback: ({status:'ok'|'error', message:payload|string}).

4. MARK_SEEN: Rate 8/sec burst 16. Payload {conversationId, messageId?}. Auth: getAuthorizedConversation. DB: Conversation.updateOne({_id}, {$set:{unreadCounts.{userId}:0}}). If messageId valid: Message.updateOne({_id:messageId, conversation:conversationId}, {$addToSet:{readBy:socket.userId}}). Broadcast socket.to(conversationId).emit(['messages_seen','messages-seen','message-seen'], {conversationId, userId:socket.userId, messageIds:messageId?[messageId][]}).

5. ADD_REACTION: Rate 8/sec burst 16. Payload {conversationId, messageId, reaction:emoji}. Auth: getAuthorizedConversation. DB: Message.findOneAndUpdate({_id:messageId, conversation:conversationId}, {$set:{reactions.{userId}:reaction}}). Broadcast io.to(conversationId).emit(['message_reaction','message-reaction'], {messageId, userId:socket.userId, reaction}).

6. REMOVE_REACTION: Rate 8/sec burst 16. Payload {conversationId, messageId}. Auth: getAuthorizedConversation. DB: Message.findOneAndUpdate({_id:messageId, conversation:conversationId}, {$unset:{reactions.{userId}:''}}). Broadcast io.to(conversationId).emit(['message_reaction','message-reaction'], {messageId, userId:socket.userId, reaction:null}).

7. TYPING_START: Rate 4/sec burst 8. Payload {conversationId:string}. Check socket.rooms.has(conversationId) (implicit auth). Broadcast socket.to(conversationId).emit(['user_typing','user-typing'], {userId:socket.userId, isTyping:true}).

8. TYPING_STOP: Rate 4/sec burst 8. Payload {conversationId:string}. Check socket.rooms.has(conversationId). Broadcast socket.to(conversationId).emit(['user_typing','user-typing'], {userId:socket.userId, isTyping:false}).

9. DELETE_MESSAGE: Rate 5/sec burst 10. Payload {messageId:ObjectId}. Auth: sender only. DB: Message.findOneAndUpdate({_id:messageId, sender:socket.userId}, {isDeleted:true, content:'This message was deleted'}, {new:false}).select('conversation'). Broadcast io.to(conversation).emit(['message_deleted','message-deleted'], {messageId}). Callback: ({status:'ok'|'error', message:string}).

10. USER_ONLINE: Rate 1/sec burst 3. Call presenceService.addConnection(socket.userId). If returns true (first connection): Conversation.find({participants:socket.userId}).select('_id').limit(500).lean(). For each: socket.to(conversationId).emit(['user_status_change','user-status-change'], {userId:socket.userId, isOnline:true}).

11. DISCONNECT: Call presenceService.removeConnection(socket.userId). If returns true (last connection): repeat conv find + broadcast with isOnline:false.

CROSS-VERSION COMPATIBILITY:
Every event registered under BOTH names: onAny(['event_name','event-name'], handler).
Every broadcast emitted to BOTH: emitRoom(room, ['event_name','event-name'], payload).
Enables old APK clients to message new app versions without updates.

RATE LIMITING:
Per-socket in-memory token buckets. Call limit(category) at handler start. Returns false if exhausted (handler bails, no DB write, no broadcast). Callback emitted every 20th rejection (category limits: message 5/sec, reaction 8/sec, typing 4/sec, presence 1/sec).

AUTHORIZATION:
getAuthorizedConversation(conversationId): Conversation.findOne({_id, participants:socket.userId}). Returns conversation if participant, null otherwise. ALL mutation handlers call this first.

SENDER PROFILE CACHING:
3-tier lookup: socket._senderProfile (socket-local) -> Redis (chat_sender:{userId}, TTL 600) -> User.findById(userId).select('username name avatar profile.avatar isVerified').lean(). Cached to prevent re-reading sender per message. Returns {_id, username, name, avatar, profile:{avatar}, isVerified}.

ROOM MEMBERSHIP:
socket.join(conversationId) ONLY in handleJoin after auth. Membership check via socket.rooms.has(conversationId) used for typing indicators (implicit auth). No other code can join conv rooms.

PAYLOAD SHAPES:
New message: {_id, conversation, sender, content, type, media, replyTo, reactions, readBy, isDeleted, createdAt, updatedAt}.
User typing: {userId, isTyping}.
User status: {userId, isOnline}.
Reaction: {messageId, userId, reaction}.
Messages seen: {conversationId, userId, messageIds}.
Message deleted: {messageId}.

FIRE-AND-FORGET PATTERN:
Conversation.updateOne() for message summary does NOT await before broadcasting. Message already persisted; failure to update summary does NOT block broadcast to clients.

files.purpose: Redis-backed presence tracking for online/offline status. Handles multi-device scenarios via connection counter. No MongoDB writes.
exports: addConnection(userId:string) -> Promise<boolean> - Increment presence, return true if first connection
removeConnection(userId:string) -> Promise<boolean> - Decrement presence, return true if last connection
touch(userId:string) -> Promise<void> - Refresh presence key TTL
isOnline(userId:string) -> Promise<boolean> - Check single user online
getOnlineSet(userIds:string[]) -> Promise<Set<string>> - Bulk check via MGET
files.schema: Redis keys: presence:{userId}='1' (EX 90), presence:count:{userId}=integer (EX 360). Multi-device: only first socket online->true, last socket offline->true. Prevents duplicate notifications when user has multiple tabs/devices.
files.details:
REDIS OPERATIONS:

addConnection(userId): INCR presence:count:{userId}. EXPIRE presence:count:{userId} 360. SET presence:{userId} '1' EX 90. Return count===1 (true only if INCR returned 1, meaning first connection). Graceful failure: catch returns false.

removeConnection(userId): DECR presence:count:{userId}. If count<=0: DEL both keys. Return count===0 (true only if DECR returned 0, last connection). Graceful failure: catch returns false.

touch(userId): SET presence:{userId} '1' EX 90 (refresh TTL without touching counter). Best-effort.

isOnline(userId): EXISTS presence:{userId}. Return true if exists.

getOnlineSet(userIds): MGET presence:{userId} for all. Return Set of user IDs where value exists. Single MGET round-trip. Graceful degradation: empty set on error.

MULTI-DEVICE SCENARIO: Phone connects (count=1, return true, notify). Browser tab connects (count=2, return false, no notify). Phone closes (count=1, return false, stays online). Browser closes (count=0, return true, notify offline). Prevents duplicate presence changes when user has multiple active connections.

files.purpose: Per-socket token-bucket rate limiting. In-memory (per-process), prevents single socket from flooding handlers.
exports: LIMITS: {message: {ratePerSec:5, burst:10}, reaction: {8,16}, typing: {4,8}, presence: {1,3}, default: {10,20}}
files.details:
TOKEN BUCKET:
TokenBucket class: tokens (starts at burst), last (Date.now), ratePerSec, burst.
Method tryRemove(now): elapsed = (now - this.last) / 1000. If elapsed > 0: tokens = min(burst, tokens + elapsed * ratePerSec); last = now. If tokens >= 1: tokens -= 1; return true. Else: return false. Refill rate continuous (smooth), not window-based.

Factory: createSocketLimiter(socket) returns guard function.
Guard(category='default'): Get config from LIMITS[category] || LIMITS.default. Get or create TokenBucket for category. Call bucket.tryRemove(Date.now()). If allowed: return true. If denied: if warned % 20 === 0: emit 'rate_limited' event {category}. Increment warned. Return false. Event emitted every 20th rejection to avoid amplifying flood.

WHY IN-MEMORY: Socket lives on ONE process for lifetime. No cross-process sharing needed. Cost: one map per socket (fine at 10K+ sockets/process). Discarded on disconnect.

WHY TOKEN BUCKET: Allows burst (up to `burst` tokens). Refills continuously (fair, smooth). Simple, client-friendly.

CALLING PATTERN: At handler start: if (!limit('category')) return; Handler bails immediately, no DB write, no broadcast. Multiple handlers share category (e.g., add_reaction + remove_reaction both 'reaction').

files.purpose: MongoDB message schema: text/media/stickers, reactions (emoji map), read receipts, replies, soft deletes.
files.schema: Collection: messages. Fields: _id (ObjectId), conversation (ref:Conversation, required), sender (ref:User, required), type (enum: text/image/video/gif/sticker/system, default: text), content (String, trim), media ({url, thumbnail, width, height, mimeType}), replyTo (ref:Message), reactions (Map<userId, emoji>, default: {}), readBy ([ObjectId], ref:User), isDeleted (Boolean, default: false), createdAt (Date auto), updatedAt (Date auto). Index: {conversation:1, createdAt:-1} for chat history queries. No hard-delete: sets isDeleted=true, content='This message was deleted'.
files.details: MongoDB schema definition. Reactions stored as Map<String, String> (userId -> emoji string). ReplyTo points to another message (FK not enforced in Mongo). ReadBy is array of user IDs who have seen this message.

files.purpose: MongoDB conversation schema: DM/group support, last message cache, unread counts per user, admin tracking.
files.schema: Collection: conversations. Fields: _id (ObjectId), type (enum: direct/group, default: direct), participants ([ObjectId], ref:User), groupName (String trim maxlength:100), groupAvatar (String), groupDescription (String maxlength:500), admins ([ObjectId], ref:User), createdBy (ObjectId, ref:User), lastMessage (ObjectId, ref:Message), lastMessageContent (String, default: 'Started a conversation'), lastMessageSender (ObjectId, ref:User), lastMessageAt (Date, default: Date.now), unreadCounts (Map<userId, count>, default: {}), createdAt (Date auto), updatedAt (Date auto). Indexes: {participants:1}, {participants:1, lastMessageAt:-1} (for conv list sorting), {lastMessageAt:-1}, {type:1}.
files.details: Unread counts model: Map of user IDs to unread message count. Set to 0 on mark_seen. Incremented for non-senders on new message. Last message cache enables listing conversations without populating full message. Allows participants to be listed without following full refs (denormalized for perf).

files.purpose: MongoDB user schema: authentication, profile, social stats, privacy, activity (subset used in realtime: username, name, avatar, isVerified).
files.schema: Relevant fields for realtime.js: _id (ObjectId), username (String, unique), name (String), avatar (String), profile.avatar (String), isVerified (Boolean). Full schema includes auth methods, social stats, privacy settings, settings, etc. Indexes on username, email, isActive, location, activity.
files.details: Sender profile caching uses User.findById(userId).select('username name avatar profile.avatar isVerified').lean(). Cached 3 ways: socket._senderProfile (connection lifetime), Redis (600s TTL), DB fallback. Lean query for speed (no Mongoose overhead).

files.purpose: Redis client wrapper with caching, single-flight getOrSet (prevents cache stampedes), rate limiting, TTL jitter.
exports: cacheService.redis: ioredis.Redis - Raw Redis client for presenceService
files.details:
REDIS CONFIG: If REDIS_URL set: new Redis(REDIS_URL, {maxRetriesPerRequest:3, enableReadyCheck:true}). Else: new Redis({host:REDIS_HOST||localhost, port:REDIS_PORT||6379, password:REDIS_PASSWORD}). Connection events: ready (isConnected=true), error (isConnected=false), close/reconnecting logged.

CACHE-ASIDE WITH SINGLE-FLIGHT: getOrSet(key, fetchFunction, ttl): Check Redis. If miss: check _inflight map. If in-flight: await that promise. If not: call fetchFunction(), cache if non-null/undefined, return. Coalesces concurrent misses: 10K requests missing same key only fetch ONCE, others await same promise. Prevents stampede on popular key expiry.

TTL JITTER: _jitter(ttl) adds random ±10% spread. Prevents synchronized expiry batch (thundering herd). Example: 600s -> 600 ± 60 = 540-660s.

METHODS USED IN REALTIME: cacheService.redis.incr(key), .expire(key, ttl), .set(key, val, 'EX', ttl), .del(key), .exists(key), .mget(keys). cacheService.set/get for JSON-serialized sender profile.

files.purpose: HTTP + Socket.IO server setup: clustering (Redis adapter), JWT auth middleware, graceful shutdown, health/readiness probes, metrics.
routes: GET /health/ready - Readiness (200 OK or 503 based on Mongo + Redis health, cached 3s)
GET /health/detailed - Full diagnostics (requires X-Internal-Key in prod)
GET /status - API summary, features, endpoints (requires X-Internal-Key in prod)
GET /metrics - Prometheus format: event_loop_lag_ms, socket_connections, socket_rooms, memory, uptime
files.details:
SOCKET.IO INIT: new Server(server, {cors: {origin: CORS_ORIGIN list, credentials:true}, pingTimeout:20000, pingInterval:25000, transports:['websocket'] or ['websocket','polling'] if SOCKET_ENABLE_POLLING=true, connectTimeout:60000, maxHttpBufferSize:1e6, perMessageDeflate:false, httpCompression:false, connectionStateRecovery:{maxDisconnectionDuration:120000, skipMiddlewares:false}}).

REDIS ADAPTER: Try to attach createAdapter(pubClient, subClient) for cross-worker event propagation. If fails: log FATAL, report to Sentry, continue with in-memory adapter (half-works).

SOCKET AUTH MIDDLEWARE: io.use((socket, next) => {token = socket.handshake.auth?.token. If missing: next(Error('Authentication required')). Else: jwtService.verifyAccessToken(token) -> decoded. socket.userId = decoded.userId. socket.user = {_id, username, name, isVerified}. On error: next(Error('Authentication failed')))}). Same verification as REST (pinned algo, issuer, audience, token type). Refresh/temp tokens cannot open sockets.

CONNECTION: io.on('connection', (socket) => {socket.join(user_{userId}). realtimeHandler(io, socket). socket.on('join-room'|'leave-room'|'join-location'...}). User room for server-side targeting. realtimeHandler attaches chat logic. Generic room join pattern-gated (location_* or user_self only).

HEALTH CHECKS: /health simple liveness. /health/ready 1s timeout Mongo+Redis, 3s cache, returns 200 if both healthy. /health/detailed full system info (memory, CPU, connections, config, database stats). All require X-Internal-Key header in production (expose infrastructure). /status endpoint lists features and links.

METRICS: Prometheus text format. pulse_event_loop_lag_ms (sampled every 1s via setInterval, measures late timer fires). pulse_socket_connections (io.engine.clientsCount). pulse_socket_rooms (io.sockets.adapter.rooms.size). Memory and uptime.

GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN: SIGTERM/SIGINT triggers gracefulShutdown(signal). Set isShuttingDown=true (health returns 503). Wait DRAIN_MS (default 5s) for LB to deregister. Emit 'server_shutdown' to sockets with reconnectInMs suggestion. io.disconnectSockets(true). server.close(). io.close(). Disconnect Mongo, Redis, SMTP. Exit 0. Hard timeout FORCE_MS (default 30s) to prevent hang. Client should add random backoff to reconnectInMs.

INIT SEQUENCE: databaseConfig.connect(). cacheService.ping() (fail loud in prod if down). firebaseConfig.initialize(). smtpConfig.initialize(). databaseConfig.createIndexes(). Start background jobs. Exit on critical failure in production.

ENV VARS: PORT, NODE_ENV, CORS_ORIGIN, SOCKET_ENABLE_POLLING, REDIS_URL, REDIS_HOST/PORT/PASSWORD, INTERNAL_STATUS_KEY, HTTP_KEEPALIVE_TIMEOUT_MS, HTTP_HEADERS_TIMEOUT_MS, HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS, SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_MS, SHUTDOWN_FORCE_MS, SENTRY_DSN.

CRITICAL AT SCALE: Redis adapter must succeed (no cross-worker propagation without it). Presence in Redis, not MongoDB (eliminates Conversation.find() on every connect). Per-socket rate limits prevent single socket flooding. Socket.IO clustering for cross-worker broadcasts. Graceful shutdown prevents reconnect storm.

specs.overview: This subsystem contains Pulse's background job scheduler, algorithm wrappers (C++-accelerated ranking engines), and core utilities. The job scheduler (scheduler.js + worker.js) manages 4 distributed cron jobs protected by Redis locks for cluster safety. The algorithm layer (feedAlgo, UserAlgo, ReelAlgo, CommentsAlgo, DNAMatchAlgo, InterestProfiler, MoodDetector, VibeClassifier) wraps native C++ addons that handle computationally expensive ranking and matching. Each wrapper gathers database signals in JS, passes them to the native addon for scoring, and falls back to a pure JS implementation if the addon is unavailable. The utilities (logger, escapeRegex, validation, helpers, constants) provide logging, regex safety, and structured data handling. The app.js file configures Express middleware (CORS, rate limiting, sanitization, compression), mounts all API routes, and defines global error handling. The cluster.js file implements Node.js clustering for multi-process scaling with graceful shutdown. The _nativeUtil.js helper normalizes Date fields to epoch milliseconds for C++ interop.

files.purpose: Background job scheduler using distributed Redis locks. Runs 4 cron jobs (engagement-decay, session-cleanup, weekly-dna-computation, trending-hashtags) on fixed intervals. Prevents double-execution in cluster mode by acquiring time-bound Redis locks. Called by server.js during initialization if ENABLE_BACKGROUND_JOBS environment variable is true.
exports: start() -> void: Initializes all 4 jobs with staggered initial delays (60-300s random per job) and repeating intervals. Stores timer handles in module-level array. Logs '[jobs] Scheduler started with N jobs: ...'
stop() -> void: Clears all stored timers and empties the array. Called on graceful shutdown.
runJob(job: {name, intervalMs, lockTtlSec, run}) -> Promise<void>: Acquires Redis lock, executes job.run() if lock obtained, logs start and completion time, catches and logs any error.
JOBS: Array of 4 job objects: {engagement-decay: DAY interval, session-cleanup: DAY interval, weekly-dna-computation: 7*DAY interval, trending-hashtags: 5*MINUTE interval}
files.details: Constants: MINUTE=60*1000, HOUR=60*MINUTE, DAY=24*HOUR. acquireLock(name, intervalMs, lockTtlSec) implementation: Tries Redis.set(`job_lock:${name}`, String(Date.now()), 'EX', ttl, 'NX') where ttl = Math.max(Math.floor(intervalMs/1000)-60, lockTtlSec). Returns true if result === 'OK' (lock acquired), false if result is null (someone else holds lock) or on Redis error (logs warning 'Lock check failed for {name}, skipping tick'). Job details: (1) engagement-decay: intervalMs=86400000 (1 day), lockTtlSec=21600 (6 hours, long enough to prevent double-fire if job runs slowly), calls UserEngagement.applyGlobalDecay(). (2) session-cleanup: intervalMs=86400000 (1 day), lockTtlSec=3600 (1 hour), calls Session.cleanupExpired(). (3) weekly-dna-computation: intervalMs=604800000 (7 days), lockTtlSec=43200 (12 hours), calls DNAMatchAlgo.runWeeklyComputation(). (4) trending-hashtags: intervalMs=300000 (5 minutes), lockTtlSec=240 (4 minutes, shorter than interval since this is cheap), lazy-requires postController.computeTrendingHashtags(), stores result in Redis with key='trending:hashtags' and TTL from env.TRENDING_HASHTAG_TTL_SEC (parseInt, default 600). Initialization stagger: for each job, setTimeout(runJob, initialDelay) then setInterval(runJob, intervalMs) where initialDelay = 60000 + Math.floor(Math.random()*240000). Timers are unref'd if supported (allows process to exit). start() called multiple times returns early if timers.length > 0. stop() called on SIGTERM/SIGINT clears intervals and empties array.

files.purpose: Standalone background worker process. Separate from the API server. Connects to MongoDB and Redis, starts the job scheduler, handles graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.
exports: main() -> Promise<void>: Entry point. Loads env via dotenv, connects database, verifies Redis availability, starts scheduler, logs completion.
shutdown(signal: string) -> Promise<void>: Handler for SIGTERM/SIGINT. Stops scheduler, disconnects database and cache, calls process.exit(0).
files.details: main() function: console.log '🛠️ Starting Pulse background worker...'. await databaseConfig.connect(). const redisOk = await cacheService.ping(). If !redisOk throw Error('Redis is required for the background worker') — Redis is critical for distributed locks. scheduler.start(). console.log '✅ Background worker running'. shutdown(signal) function: console.log '\n🛑 {signal} received. Shutting down worker...'. scheduler.stop(). await databaseConfig.disconnect(). await cacheService.disconnect(). process.exit(0). Top-level process.on('SIGTERM', () => shutdown('SIGTERM')). process.on('SIGINT', () => shutdown('SIGINT')). main().catch(err => {console.error('❌ Worker startup failed:', err); process.exit(1);}). Usage: node -r dotenv/config src/jobs/worker.js

files.purpose: Structured logging using Winston. Outputs to console (colorized, short time format) and rotating file streams (error.log, combined.log, exceptions.log). Used throughout codebase for centralized logging.
exports: logger: winston.Logger instance with level, format, transports, exception handlers, rejection handlers configured
files.details: Winston Logger configuration: level = process.env.LOG_LEVEL || (NODE_ENV==='production' ? 'warn' : 'info'). Format: combine(errors({stack:true}), timestamp({format:'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss'}), custom printf). Custom printf: `${timestamp} [${level}]: ${stack || message}` + metadata if any. defaultMeta: {service: 'pulse-backend'}. Transports: (1) Console transport with colorize() + timestamp({format:'HH:mm:ss'}) + logFormat. (2) File transport (logs/error.log) level='error' only, maxsize=5242880 (5MB), maxFiles=5. (3) File transport (logs/combined.log) all levels, maxsize=10485760 (10MB), maxFiles=3. exceptionHandlers: [File({filename:'logs/exceptions.log'})]. rejectionHandlers: [File({filename:'logs/rejections.log'})]. No crash on unhandled exceptions — logged to file instead.

files.purpose: Sanitize user input before use in MongoDB regex queries. Escapes all regex metacharacters to prevent ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) and unintended pattern matches.
exports: escapeRegex(str?: string) -> string: Escapes regex metacharacters in the input string
files.details: Function: module.exports = function escapeRegex(str = '') { return String(str).replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'); }. Takes optional str (default ''). Converts to String. Replaces matches of regex metacharacters (.*+?^${}()|[]\) with backslash-escaped versions. Example: 'hello.world' -> 'hello\\.world'. Used before passing user search input to MongoDB $regex operator. Prevents patterns like '(a+)+$' from causing ReDoS.

files.purpose: Express application factory. Configures all middleware, mounts route handlers, and defines global error handling. Called by server.js to create the HTTP server.
exports: app: Express application instance with all middleware and routes configured
files.details: Middleware stack (in order): (1) helmet({contentSecurityPolicy:false, crossOriginResourcePolicy:{policy:'cross-origin'}}). (2) cors() with custom origin callback: allows requests with no origin (mobile), checks web origins against CORS_ORIGIN env (split by comma) or hardcoded list [localhost:5174/3000/5173/3100], logs warnings for rejected origins. (3) compression(). (4) globalLimiter (rate limit middleware from ./middlewares/rateLimit). (5) morgan('short' in prod, 'combined' in dev, skipped in test NODE_ENV). (6) express.json(limit=env.JSON_BODY_LIMIT || '256kb'). (7) express.urlencoded(extended=true, limit=same). (8) sanitizeBody (from ./middlewares/sanitize, XSS prevention). (9) Pagination clamping: req.query.limit parsed and clamped to [1,100], default 20. (10) Request metadata: req.timestamp = new Date(), req.requestId = crypto.randomBytes(8).toString('hex'). (11) Production 5xx scrubber (if NODE_ENV==='production'): wraps res.json to intercept status >= 500 and replace body with {success:false, error:'An unexpected internal server error occurred.', code: body?.code || 'INTERNAL_ERROR', requestId}. Trust proxy: app.set('trust proxy', parseInt(TRUST_PROXY_HOPS) || 1). Sentry initialization: if SENTRY_DSN env set, Sentry.init({dsn, environment, tracesSampleRate: prod?0.2:1.0}). Route mounts: /api/v1/auth, /api/v1/users, /api/v1/posts, /api/v1/feed, /api/v1/chat, /api/v1/media, /api/v1/gifs, /api/v1/reels, /api/v1/snaps, /api/v1/groups, /api/v1/admin, /api/v1/whispers, /api/v1/pulse-drops, /api/v1/chains, /api/v1/alter-ego, /api/v1/notifications, /api/v1/push, /api/v1/social-dna, /api/v1/pulse-score, /api/v1/roulette, /api/v1/bookmarks, /api/v1/referral. /share (public OG routes, no auth). /api-docs Swagger (conditional: shown unless NODE_ENV==='production' && ENABLE_API_DOCS !== 'true'). /api-docs.json returns swagger spec JSON. 404 handler: (req,res,next) => res.status(404).json({success:false, message:'Route not found. The requested endpoint does not exist.', path:req.originalUrl, method:req.method, timestamp:ISO}). Global error handler: (error,req,res,next) => {if error.name==='ValidationError' return 400 with {success:false, error:'Validation failed...', details:errors array, code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'}. if error.code===11000 return 409 with {success:false, error:'A record with that {field} already exists.', code:'DUPLICATE_ERROR'}. else status = error.status || 500. safeMessage = (status >= 500 && prod) ? 'An unexpected internal server error occurred.' : error.message. return {success:false, error:safeMessage, code:error.code||'INTERNAL_ERROR', requestId, ...(dev && {stack:error.stack})}. Sentry error handler installed if DSN.
routes: GET /health -> {status:'OK', timestamp:ISO8601, service:'Pulse Backend API', version:'1.0.0'}
GET /* (404) -> {success:false, message:'Route not found', path, method, timestamp}

files.purpose: Node.js cluster mode orchestration. Primary process forks N worker processes, handles worker crashes with auto-restart, implements graceful shutdown.
files.details: Module-level: const WORKER_COUNT = parseInt(CLUSTER_WORKERS) || os.cpus().length. if (cluster.isPrimary): logs '🏗️ Pulse Cluster Master (PID:{pid})' and CPU count. Forks WORKER_COUNT workers. Listens to cluster events: (1) 'online': logs '✅ Worker {id} online (PID:{pid})'. (2) 'exit': if isShuttingDown=true logs '🔌 Worker {id} exited', checks if all workers gone and exits master with exit(0). else logs '💀 Worker {id} died ({reason}). Restarting in 2s...' and setTimeout(cluster.fork, 2000) if !isShuttingDown. Graceful shutdown function (called on SIGTERM/SIGINT): if isShuttingDown return early. isShuttingDown=true. logs '🛑 {signal} received. Shutting down all workers...'. for (const id in cluster.workers) workers[id].process.kill(signal). setTimeout(() => {console.error('⏰ Forced shutdown after worker drain timeout'); process.exit(1);}, SHUTDOWN_FORCE_MS + 5000) where SHUTDOWN_FORCE_MS = parseInt(SHUTDOWN_FORCE_MS) || 30000. else (worker): require('./server'). process.on('SIGTERM', () => shutdown('SIGTERM')). process.on('SIGINT', () => shutdown('SIGINT')).

files.purpose: Helpers for JavaScript-to-C++ boundary. Normalizes Date fields to epoch milliseconds before JSON serialization, since C++ addon expects timestamps as numeric epoch ms, not ISO strings.
exports: toMs(v: any) -> number|any: Converts Date/ISO string to epoch milliseconds. Passes through null, numbers unchanged. Handles Date objects and date strings.
msFields(items: Array, dateFields?: string[], nestedArrayFields?: string[]) -> Array: Shallow-clones array, converts specified date fields to ms, recursively processes nested arrays.
files.details: toMs(v) logic: if (v == null) return v. if (typeof v === 'number') return v. if (v instanceof Date) return v.getTime(). else {const t = new Date(v).getTime(); return Number.isNaN(t) ? v : t;}. msFields(items, dateFields=['createdAt'], nestedArrayFields=[]) logic: if (!Array.isArray(items)) return items. return items.map(item => {if (!item || typeof item !== 'object') return item. const copy = {...item}. for (const f of dateFields) if (copy[f] !== undefined) copy[f] = toMs(copy[f]). for (const nf of nestedArrayFields) if (Array.isArray(copy[nf])) copy[nf] = msFields(copy[nf], dateFields, nestedArrayFields). return copy.}). Used before JSON.stringify() before passing to native addon. Example: msFields(posts, ['createdAt']) converts all posts[i].createdAt from Date objects to ms. Example: msFields(comments, ['createdAt'], ['replies']) also recurses into each comment's replies array.

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for post feed ranking. Gathers database signals in JavaScript (affinity, velocity, social proof, interest relevance, trust scores), passes to native addon for scoring, falls back to pure JS if addon unavailable. Public API unchanged.
exports: rankPosts(posts: Array<Post>, userId?: ObjectId, options?: {followingIds?, mutualIds?, friendIds?, trendingHashtags?, includeVelocity?}) -> Promise<Array<Post>>: Ranks posts by personalization, engagement velocity, social proof, freshness. Returns empty array if no posts.
rankPostsWithVibe(posts, userId, options) -> Promise<Array>: Classifies post vibes, optionally filters/boosts by vibe, then calls rankPosts.
getForYouFeed(userId, posts, options) -> Promise<Array>: Filters out user's own posts, calls rankPosts for personalized feed.
getTrendingPosts(posts, options) -> Promise<Array>: Routes to pure JS impl (not accelerated; velocity-based trending).

Plus exports from _fallback JS impl: calculatePostScore, applyTimeDecay, getTrendingVelocity, getHashtagBoost, assignVRRCategory, applyVRRDistribution, applySessionPacing, applySocialProof, injectCuriosityGaps, applyFreshness, getMediaType, classifyPostVibes, filterByVibe, applyContentFatigue, enforceAuthorDiversity, applyNegativeSignals, applyQualityGate, applyEngagementFeedback, applyColdStartBoost, CONFIG
files.details: rankPosts(posts, userId, options={}) signature: async function. return [] if !posts?.length. if (!addon) return JS.rankPosts(posts, userId, options) [pure JS fallback]. else try: (1) Destructure options: {followingIds=[], mutualIds=[], friendIds=[], trendingHashtags=[], includeVelocity=true}. (2) Fetch user behavior: if (userId) try { userBehavior = await UserBehavior.getPreferences(userId); sessionDepth = userBehavior.sessionDepth || 0; seenPostIds = await UserBehavior.getSeenPostIds(userId, 24) [24-hour lookback]; isColdStart = userBehavior.totalInteractions < JS.CONFIG.COLD_START.MIN_INTERACTIONS; } catch { isColdStart=true; } else isColdStart=true. (3) Fetch batch affinity scores: const authorIds = [...new Set(posts.map(p => p.author?._id || p.author).filter(Boolean))]; const affinityCacheMap = userId ? await UserEngagement.getBatchAffinities(userId, authorIds) : new Map(); convert Map to object. (4) Fetch velocity: if (includeVelocity) { const vMap = await Like.getBatchLikeVelocities('post', posts.map(p => p._id), JS.CONFIG.VELOCITY_WINDOW_HOURS); convert Map to object velocityMap. }. (5) Fetch social proof (friend likes): friendLikes = {}; if (friendIds?.length) { const likes = await Like.find({user:{$in:friendIds}, targetType:'post', targetId:{$in:posts.map(String)._id}}).select('targetId').lean(); for (const l of likes) friendLikes[l.targetId.toString()] = (friendLikes[...] || 0) + 1; }. (6) Fetch interest relevance: relevanceMap = {}; if (userId && userBehavior) try { const scored = await InterestProfiler.batchScorePosts(posts, userId); for (const s of scored) relevanceMap[(s._id || s).toString()] = s._relevanceScore; } catch {}. (7) Fetch trust/bait signals: try { ({trustMap, baitMap} = await trustService.buildSignals(posts)); } catch {}. (8) Build JSON payload: {posts: msFields(posts, ['createdAt']), userId: userId?.toString() || null, nowMs: Date.now(), trustMap, baitMap, followingIds: followingIds.map(String), mutualIds: mutualIds.map(String), friendIds: friendIds.map(String), trendingHashtags, velocityMap, affinityMap, friendLikes, relevanceMap, seenPostIds:[...seenPostIds], isColdStart, sessionDepth}. (9) Call native: JSON.parse(addon.feedRank(JSON.stringify(payload))). (10) Return ranked posts array. On error: log '[feedAlgo] native path failed, using JS fallback: {err.message}'; return JS.rankPosts(posts, userId, options). rankPostsWithVibe(posts, userId, options={}): destructure vibe='auto' from options, spread rest. For each post: if post.vibe && post.vibe !== 'general' keep, else call VibeClassifier.classify(post) to get {vibe, vibeScore, confidence} and spread onto post. If vibe && vibe !== 'auto': filter posts where post.vibe === vibe || post.vibeScore[vibe] > 1.5. Call VibeClassifier.boostByVibe(posts, vibe, 1.5). Call rankPosts(posts, userId, rest). getForYouFeed(userId, posts, options): filter posts to exclude author === userId. Call rankPosts(filtered, userId, options). getTrendingPosts: JS.getTrendingPosts (not accelerated).

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for user recommendations (suggested users, similar users, trending creators). Gathers affinities and graph proximity in JS, runs scoring in native addon, falls back to pure JS.
exports: getSuggestedUsers(userId, candidateUsers, options?) -> Promise<Array<User>>: Returns recommended users ranked by affinity + graph proximity + diversity. Filters out self and users already followed.
getSimilarUsers(targetUser, candidateUsers, limit=10) -> Promise<Array<User>>: Routes to native or JS implementation.
getTrendingCreators(users, options?) -> Array<User>: Pure trending ranking by follower count and engagement.

Plus exports from JS impl: calculateCreatorScore, calculateEngagementRate, calculateContentSimilarity, calculateGraphProximity, getInterestAffinity, getBatchAffinities, injectDiversity, isNicheCreator, CONFIG
files.details: getSuggestedUsers(userId, candidateUsers, options={}): if (!addon) return JS.getSuggestedUsers(...). else: (1) Destructure options: {followingIds=[], mutualFollows=[], followingFollowing={}, limit=20}. (2) Filter candidates: const followingSet = new Set(followingIds.map(String)); candidates = candidateUsers.filter(u => uid(u) !== userId?.toString() && !followingSet.has(uid(u))); if (candidates.length === 0) return []. (3) Get batch affinities: const affinityCacheMap = await JS.getBatchAffinities(userId, candidateIds); convert to object. (4) Calculate graph proximity: const graphMap = JS.calculateGraphProximity(userId, candidates, followingIds, followingFollowing); convert to object graphScores. (5) Compute cold start: isColdStart = followingIds.length < JS.CONFIG.COLD_START.MIN_FOLLOWING. (6) Build payload: {mode:'suggested', userId: userId?.toString() || '', candidates, followingIds: map(String), mutualFollows: map(String), affinityMap, graphScores, isColdStart, limit, nowMs: Date.now()}. (7) Call native: const scored = JSON.parse(addon.userRank(JSON.stringify(payload))). (8) Inject diversity (JS, preserves Math.random behavior): const diversityRate = isColdStart ? JS.CONFIG.RECOMMENDATION_DIVERSITY + JS.CONFIG.COLD_START.DIVERSITY_BOOST : JS.CONFIG.RECOMMENDATION_DIVERSITY; return JS.injectDiversity(scored, limit, diversityRate).slice(0, limit). Fallback on error. getSimilarUsers(targetUser, candidateUsers, limit=10): if (addon) try { payload = {mode:'similar', target:targetUser, candidates:candidateUsers, limit, nowMs:Date.now()}; return JSON.parse(addon.userRank(JSON.stringify(payload))); } catch { log and fallback }. return JS.getSimilarUsers(...). getTrendingCreators(users, options={}): if (addon) try { payload = {mode:'trending', candidates:users, limit:options.limit||10, minFollowers:options.minFollowers||100, nowMs:Date.now()}; return JSON.parse(addon.userRank(JSON.stringify(payload))); } catch {}. return JS.getTrendingCreators(users, options).

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for short-form video (reel) feed ranking. Gathers velocity and affinity in JS, runs ranking in native addon. Implements diversity injection post-ranking.
exports: rankReels(reels, userId, options?) -> Promise<Array<Reel>>: Ranks reels by engagement + freshness + personalization + audio preference.
getForYouFeed(userId, reels, options?) -> Promise<Array>: Filters out user's own reels, calls rankReels with diversity injection enabled.
getFollowingFeed(userId, reels, followingIds) -> Promise<Array>: Returns only reels from followed creators, rankReels without velocity.

Plus exports from JS: calculateEngagementScore, applyTimeDecay, getFreshnessBoost, getPersonalizationBoost, getCreatorScore, calculateVelocity, injectDiversity, getAudioBoost, getCreatorColdStartBoost, applyReelSessionPacing, enforceCategoryDiversity, CONFIG
files.details: rankReels(reels, userId, options={}): return [] if !reels?.length. if (!addon) return JS.rankReels(...). else: (1) Destructure options: {includeVelocity=true, followingIds=[], sessionDepth=0, userAudioPrefs={}, negativeSignals={}}. (2) Get batch affinities: authorIds = [...new Set(reels.map(r => r.user?._id || r.user || r.author?._id || r.author).filter(Boolean))]; affinityCacheMap = userId ? await UserEngagement.getBatchAffinities(userId, authorIds) : new Map(); convert to object. (3) Get velocity: if (includeVelocity) { vMap = await Like.getBatchLikeVelocities('reel', reels.map(r => r._id), JS.CONFIG.VELOCITY_WINDOW_HOURS); convert to object. }. (4) Build payload: {reels: msFields(reels, ['createdAt']), userId: userId?.toString() || null, nowMs: Date.now(), followingIds: map(String), velocityMap, affinityMap, userAudioPrefs, negativeSignals:{skippedCreators: (negativeSignals.skippedCreators||[]).map(String), hiddenCategories: (negativeSignals.hiddenCategories||[]).map(String)}, sessionDepth}. (5) Call native: result = JSON.parse(addon.reelRank(JSON.stringify(payload))). (6) Optionally inject diversity: if (options.injectDiversityContent !== false && reels.length > 10) result = JS.injectDiversity(result, reels). (7) Return result. Fallback on error. getForYouFeed(userId, reels, options): filter reels excluding user's own. const discovery = others.length >= 5 ? others : reels [fallback to all if few others]. return rankReels(discovery, userId, {...options, injectDiversityContent: true}). getFollowingFeed(userId, reels, followingIds): set = new Set(followingIds.map(String)); followed = reels.filter(r => set.has(aid(r)) || aid(r) === userId?.toString()); return rankReels(followed, userId, {includeVelocity:false, injectDiversityContent:false, followingIds}).

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for comment ranking. Runs regex-heavy quality, spam, and toxicity detection in native addon. Handles recursive reply ranking.
exports: rankComments(comments, options?) -> Promise<Array<Comment>>: Ranks comments by quality (substance, engagement), toxicity (spam, abuse), and recursive replies. Returns ranked flattened or threaded structure.

Plus exports from JS: calculateCommentQuality, applyTimeDecay, calculateControversy, getReplyThread, flattenThread, getTopCommentsPreview, isSpammy, filterLowQuality, detectToxicity, analyzeSubstance, wilsonScore, analyzeReplyChainQuality, CONFIG
files.details: rankComments(comments, options={}): return [] if !comments || comments.length === 0. if (addon) try { payload = {comments: msFields(comments, ['createdAt'], ['replies']), mode: options.mode || JS.CONFIG.SORT_MODES.BEST, opId: options.opId?.toString() || '', nowMs: Date.now()}; return JSON.parse(addon.commentsRank(JSON.stringify(payload))); } catch (err) { log '[CommentsAlgo] native path failed, using JS fallback: {err.message}'; }. return JS.rankComments(comments, options). Payload recursively normalizes createdAt in comments and replies arrays. opId (original post/parent author) used to suppress self-promotional comments. mode options: e.g., JS.CONFIG.SORT_MODES.BEST, CONTROVERSIAL, etc.

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for Social DNA matching (user compatibility, twin matching, weekly precomputation). Implements full-scan prevention in findTwins by capping candidate set and serving precomputed twins first. Uses keyset cursor pagination in runWeeklyComputation to avoid expensive skip().
exports: recordInteraction(userId, post, action='like') -> Promise<void>: Records user engagement for DNA profile building. Routes to JS implementation.
getCompatibility(userIdA, userIdB) -> Promise<{matchPercent, confidence, ...}>: Fetches both DNAs, scores compatibility in native addon.
findTwins(userId, limit?) -> Promise<Array<{user, matchPercent}>>: Returns matching twin users. Serves precomputed twins first (from weekly job), caps candidate scan to 1000, returns max 50 twins.
calculateMatchPercent(strandsA, strandsB) -> number: Pure cosine similarity between DNA strands (0-100).
runWeeklyComputation() -> Promise<{processed: number}>: Keyset cursor batch processor. Iterates all DNA docs, snapshots each, saves insights. Processed count returned.
files.details: Constants: MAX_CANDIDATES_SCANNED=1000 (prevents full-collection scans), MAX_TWINS_RETURNED=50. Helper: interactionCountOf(dna) = dna.interactionCount ?? dna.totalSignals [uses real interaction count if present, falls back to weighted signal sum]. recordInteraction: routes entirely to JS.recordInteraction(userId, post, action). getCompatibility(userIdA, userIdB): Promise.all([SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userIdA), SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userIdB)]). if (addon) try { payload = {mode:'compatibility', a:{strands:dnaA.strands, totalSignals:dnaA.totalSignals, dominantVibe:dnaA.dominantVibe, interactionCount:interactionCountOf(dnaA)}, b:{same for dnaB}}; return JSON.parse(addon.dnaMatch(JSON.stringify(payload))); } catch { log and fallback }. return JS.getCompatibility(userIdA, userIdB). findTwins(userId, limit=JS.CONFIG.MAX_TWINS): safeLimit = Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(limit)||JS.CONFIG.MAX_TWINS, 1), MAX_TWINS_RETURNED) [clamp to safe range]. const userDNA = await SocialDNA.getOrCreate(userId). if (userDNA.totalSignals < JS.CONFIG.MIN_SIGNALS_FOR_MATCH) return []. Step 1 (precomputed): if (Array.isArray(userDNA.twins) && userDNA.twins.length > 0) { const populated = await SocialDNA.populate(userDNA, {path:'twins.user', select:'username profile.displayName profile.avatar isVerified'}).catch(()=>null); const list = (populated?.twins || userDNA.twins).filter(t=>t.user).slice(0, safeLimit).map(t=>({user:t.user, matchPercent:t.matchPercent})); if (list.length) return list; }. Step 2 (on-demand): let candidates = await SocialDNA.find({user:{$ne:userId}, dominantVibe:userDNA.dominantVibe, totalSignals:{$gte:JS.CONFIG.MIN_SIGNALS_FOR_MATCH}}).limit(MAX_CANDIDATES_SCANNED).populate('user', ...).lean(). if (candidates.length < safeLimit) { const extra = await SocialDNA.find({user:{$ne:userId}, totalSignals:{$gte:JS.CONFIG.MIN_SIGNALS_FOR_MATCH}}).limit(MAX_CANDIDATES_SCANNED - candidates.length).populate('user', ...).lean(); const seen = new Set(candidates.map(c=>c._id.toString())); for (const e of extra) if (!seen.has(e._id.toString())) candidates.push(e); }. If (addon) try { payload = {mode:'batch', user:{strands:userDNA.strands, totalSignals:userDNA.totalSignals, dominantVibe:userDNA.dominantVibe, interactionCount:interactionCountOf(userDNA)}, candidates: candidates.map(c=>({user:c.user, strands:c.strands, totalSignals:c.totalSignals, dominantVibe:c.dominantVibe, interactionCount:interactionCountOf(c)}})), limit:safeLimit}; return JSON.parse(addon.dnaMatch(JSON.stringify(payload))); } catch {}. JS fallback: const matches = []; for (const c of candidates) { mp = JS.calculateMatchPercent(userDNA.strands, c.strands); if (mp < 50) continue; conf = JS.calculateConfidence(interactionCountOf(userDNA), interactionCountOf(c)); mutual = userDNA.dominantVibe === c.dominantVibe; matches.push({user:c.user, matchPercent: Math.min(100, mp + (mutual?3:0)), confidence:conf}); }. matches.sort((a,b) => (b.matchPercent*(0.7+b.confidence*0.3)) - (a.matchPercent*(0.7+a.confidence*0.3))). return matches.slice(0, safeLimit). runWeeklyComputation(): console.log '[DNAMatchAlgo] Starting weekly DNA computation (keyset)...'. const filter = {totalSignals:{$gte:1}}, batchSize = JS.CONFIG.WEEKLY_BATCH_SIZE, processed = 0, lastId = null. for (;;) { const q = lastId ? {...filter, _id:{$gt:lastId}} : filter; const batch = await SocialDNA.find(q).sort({_id:1}).limit(batchSize); if (batch.length === 0) break; const results = await Promise.allSettled(batch.map(async dna => { dna.takeSnapshot(); dna.latestInsights = dna.snapshots[dna.snapshots.length-1]?.insights || []; await dna.save(); return true; })); processed += results.filter(r=>r.status==='fulfilled').length; lastId = batch[batch.length-1]._id; }. console.log '[DNAMatchAlgo] Weekly computation complete. Processed {processed} users.'. return {processed}.

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for post relevance scoring (interest-based personalization). DB-bound entry points fetch user preferences in JS, run pure relevance scoring in native addon. Lightweight pure helpers stay in JS.
exports: getRelevanceScore(post, userId, userPrefs?) -> Promise<number>: Returns 1.0 if no post/userId, else relevance score [0-max] indicating how well post matches user interests.
batchScorePosts(posts, userId) -> Promise<Array<Post>>: Returns posts with _relevanceScore and _isSeen fields populated.

Plus pure JS helpers: extractTopics, calculateTopicMatch, calculateNichenessBoost, calculateCrossTopicBoost, getMediaType, getPostLengthKey, getTopInterests, TOPIC_PATTERNS, CROSS_TOPIC_AFFINITIES, CONFIG
files.details: Helper normalizePrefs(prefs): converts Mongo Map/object prefs to plain JSON. topics = {} [loop Map or copy object]. mediaTypes = prefs?.mediaTypes || {}. postLengths = prefs?.postLengths || {}. authorAffinities = {} [same Map/object logic] or null if empty. return {topics, mediaTypes, postLengths, authorAffinities}. getRelevanceScore(post, userId, userPrefs=null): return 1.0 if (!post || !userId). prefs = userPrefs || await UserBehavior.getPreferences(userId). if (addon) try { payload = {posts:[post], prefs:normalizePrefs(prefs)}; res = JSON.parse(addon.interestScore(JSON.stringify(payload))); if (Array.isArray(res) && res.length) return res[0].relevance; } catch {}. return JS.getRelevanceScore(post, userId, prefs). batchScorePosts(posts, userId): return posts if empty. prefs = await UserBehavior.getPreferences(userId). seenIds = await UserBehavior.getSeenPostIds(userId, 24). if (addon) try { payload = {posts, prefs:normalizePrefs(prefs)}; scored = JSON.parse(addon.interestScore(JSON.stringify(payload))); byId = new Map(scored.map(s=>[s.postId, s.relevance])); return posts.map(post => {id = (post._id || post).toString(); rel = byId.get(id) ?? 1.0; if (seenIds.has(id)) rel *= JS.CONFIG.SEEN_PENALTY; return {...post, _relevanceScore:rel, _isSeen:seenIds.has(id)};}); } catch {}. return JS.batchScorePosts(posts, userId).

files.purpose: Stateful mood detection wrapper. Kept as pure JS implementation due to statefulness (per-instance momentum + EMA smoothing). Not amenable to stateless C++ kernel. Native addon available for future pure scoring if needed.
exports: MoodDetector: class exported directly from _fallback/MoodDetector.js with stateful methods
files.details: Module exports JSImpl directly from _fallback/MoodDetector. Addon is require'd at top (so native module loads once) but not used. This preserves exact public behavior: stateful momentum, EMA history, content.text fix. Canonical implementation is the JS class; any future pure kernel work would add optional acceleration without breaking the interface.

files.purpose: C++-accelerated wrapper for vibe/mood classification. Pure classification math (text analysis, pattern matching) runs in native addon. Light array operations (filterByVibe, boostByVibe) stay in JS to preserve random seeding and exact behavior.
exports: VibeClassifier: class with static methods classify(post), classifyBatch(posts), filterByVibe(posts, vibe, minConfidence=0.2), boostByVibe(posts, vibe, boostFactor=1.5)
files.details: VibeClassifier.classify(post): if (addon) try { return JSON.parse(addon.vibeClassify(JSON.stringify(post || {}))); } catch {} return JS.classify(post). VibeClassifier.classifyBatch(posts): if (addon) try { return JSON.parse(addon.vibeClassify(JSON.stringify(posts || []))); } catch {} return JS.classifyBatch(posts). Both methods fall back to JS on error or if addon unavailable. filterByVibe(posts, vibe, minConfidence=0.2): keeps JS version; it calls this.classify() per post which routes through addon if available, preserving identical behavior. Logic: posts.filter(p => p.vibe === vibe || (p.vibeScore && p.vibeScore[vibe] > minConfidence)). boostByVibe(posts, vibe, boostFactor=1.5): keeps JS version. Logic: posts.map(p => (p.vibe === vibe || ...) ? {...p, score: p.score * boostFactor} : p).
